+
+function normalizeLanguage(className = "") {
+  const match = String(className).match(/\blanguage-([\w-]+)/);
+  return match?.[1] || "text";
+}
+
+function CodeBlock({ className, code }) {
+  const language = useMemo(() => normalizeLanguage(className), [className]);
+  const [html, setHtml] = useState("");
+  const [copied, setCopied] = useState(false);
+
+  useEffect(() => {
+    let active = true;
+
+    async function renderHighlightedCode() {
+      try {
+        const { codeToHtml } = await import("shiki");
+        const nextHtml = await codeToHtml(code, {
+          lang: language,
+          theme: "github-light"
+        });
+
+        if (active) {
+          setHtml(nextHtml);
+        }
+      } catch {
+        if (active) {
+          setHtml("");
+        }
+      }
+    }
+
+    void renderHighlightedCode();
+
+    return () => {
+      active = false;
+    };
+  }, [code, language]);
+
+  async function copyCode() {
+    try {
+      await navigator.clipboard?.writeText(code);
+      setCopied(true);
+      window.setTimeout(() => setCopied(false), 1200);
+    } catch {
+      setCopied(false);
+    }
+  }
+
+  return (
+    <div className="chat-code-block">
+      <div className="chat-code-toolbar">
+        <span>{language}</span>
+        <button type="button" onClick={copyCode}>
+          {copied ? "Copied" : "Copy"}
+        </button>
+      </div>
+      <div
+        className="chat-code-shiki"
+        dangerouslySetInnerHTML={{
+          __html:
+            html ||
+            `<pre><code>${code
+              .replace(/&/g, "&amp;")
+              .replace(/</g, "&lt;")
+              .replace(/>/g, "&gt;")}</code></pre>`
+        }}
+      />
+    </div>
+  );
+}

@@
 function isDefaultStyle(style) {
   return sameStyle(style, INLINE_DEFAULTS);
 }
+
+function cssColorToWpsColor(color) {
+  const match = String(color || "").trim().match(/^#?([0-9a-f]{6})$/i);
+  if (!match) {
+    return null;
+  }
+
+  const value = match[1];
+  const red = Number.parseInt(value.slice(0, 2), 16);
+  const green = Number.parseInt(value.slice(2, 4), 16);
+  const blue = Number.parseInt(value.slice(4, 6), 16);
+  return red + green * 256 + blue * 65536;
+}
+
+function normalizeCodeLanguage(language) {
+  const normalized = String(language || "").trim().toLowerCase();
+  if (!normalized) {
+    return "";
+  }
+
+  const aliases = {
+    bash: "bash",
+    cjs: "javascript",
+    cmd: "dos",
+    js: "javascript",
+    jsx: "javascript",
+    mjs: "javascript",
+    ps1: "powershell",
+    py: "python",
+    shell: "bash",
+    sh: "bash",
+    ts: "typescript",
+    tsx: "typescript",
+    yml: "yaml"
+  };
+
+  return aliases[normalized] || normalized;
+}
+
+function codeTokenColor(className, inheritedColor = null) {
+  const classes = String(className || "").split(/\s+/);
+  for (const item of classes) {
+    const token = item.replace(/^hljs-/, "");
+    const color = CODE_TOKEN_COLORS[token];
+    if (color) {
+      return cssColorToWpsColor(color);
+    }
+  }
+
+  return inheritedColor;
+}
+
+function collectHighlightedRuns(node, runs, inheritedColor = null) {
+  if (!node) {
+    return;
+  }
+
+  if (node.nodeType === 3) {
+    const text = node.nodeValue || "";
+    if (text) {
+      runs.push({ color: inheritedColor, text });
+    }
+    return;
+  }
+
+  const nextColor =
+    node.nodeType === 1 ? codeTokenColor(node.getAttribute("class"), inheritedColor) : inheritedColor;
+
+  for (const child of Array.from(node.childNodes || [])) {
+    collectHighlightedRuns(child, runs, nextColor);
+  }
+}
+
+function createHighlightedCodeRuns(text, language) {
+  const source = String(text ?? "");
+  if (!source) {
+    return [];
+  }
+
+  if (typeof document === "undefined") {
+    return [{ color: null, text: source }];
+  }
+
+  try {
+    const normalizedLanguage = normalizeCodeLanguage(language);
+    const result =
+      normalizedLanguage && hljs.getLanguage(normalizedLanguage)
+        ? hljs.highlight(source, {
+            ignoreIllegals: true,
+            language: normalizedLanguage
+          })
+        : hljs.highlightAuto(source);
+
+    const root = document.createElement("div");
+    root.innerHTML = result.value;
+    const runs = [];
+    collectHighlightedRuns(root, runs);
+    return runs.length > 0 ? runs : [{ color: null, text: source }];
+  } catch {
+    return [{ color: null, text: source }];
+  }
+}
+
+function activeCodeBlockContext(state) {
+  return findLastContext(
+    state,
+    (entry) => entry.type === CODE_BLOCK || entry.type === CODE_FENCE
+  );
+}
```
</details></tool-use>

# RTK - Rust Token Killer (Codex CLI)

**Usage**: Token-optimized CLI proxy for shell commands.

## Rule

Always prefix shell commands with `rtk`.

Examples:

```bash
rtk git status
rtk cargo test
rtk npm run build
rtk pytest -q
```

## Meta Commands

```bash
rtk gain            # Token savings analytics
rtk gain --history  # Recent command savings history
rtk proxy <cmd>     # Run raw command without filtering
```

## Verification

```bash
rtk --version
rtk gain
which rtk
```
```
</details></tool-use>

---
name: rtk-usage
description: Use when working with `rtk` on Windows or Codex, especially for command selection, validating `rtk` behavior, fixing `rtk` failures, diagnosing `No hook installed`, or avoiding incorrect fallback to raw shell commands. Trigger for `rtk` installation, upgrade, init, hook, rewrite, proxy, env, json, verify, gain, and command-usage troubleshooting.
---

# RTK Usage

Use this skill whenever a task depends on `rtk` working correctly.

## Core Rule

1. If an `rtk` command fails, do not fall back to the equivalent raw shell command.
2. Diagnose and fix the `rtk` issue first.
3. Only continue the main task after `rtk` is working again.
4. Prefer RTK-native subcommands over `rtk run` / `rtk proxy` when a native subcommand exists.
5. On Windows, quote carefully in the outer shell; many apparent RTK failures are PowerShell quoting errors.
6. If a non-interactive command can be run through `rtk`, run it through `rtk`.
7. Raw shell is allowed only when exact byte-for-byte output matters, the command is tiny/trivial, the command is interactive, or `rtk` would change semantics.
8. When updating this skill, add only correct reusable RTK command forms and decision rules.

## Windows Baseline Checks

1. Run `rtk --version`.
2. Run `rtk --help`.
3. Run `rtk gain`.
4. Run `rtk init -g --show` for Claude hook status.
5. Run `rtk init -g --codex --show` for Codex global status.
6. Run `rtk verify`.
7. Run `rtk proxy where.exe rtk` to confirm the active binary.
8. On Windows release installs, prefer the official GitHub asset `rtk-x86_64-pc-windows-msvc.zip`.

Known-good Windows baseline observed for `rtk 0.37.2`:

```bash
rtk --version
rtk --help
rtk gain
rtk verify
rtk init -g --show
rtk init -g --codex --show
rtk proxy where.exe rtk
```

## Correct Command Patterns

Use these forms exactly:

```bash
rtk env --filter PATH
rtk json package.json
rtk rewrite "git status --short"
rtk proxy git status --short
rtk proxy powershell -NoProfile -Command "Get-Content package.json -TotalCount 20"
rtk powershell -NoProfile -Command "Get-Content package.json -TotalCount 20"
rtk run "git branch --show-current"
rtk run -c "git status --short"
rtk verify
rtk help hook
rtk hook check git status --short
```

Rules:

1. `rtk env` uses `--filter`; do not pass variable names as positional arguments.
2. `rtk json` takes a JSON file path; do not pass inline JSON text.
3. `rtk rewrite` can exit non-zero even after printing a valid rewrite in some Windows builds; judge by printed output and `rtk rewrite --help`, not by code alone.
4. `rtk proxy` is acceptable for diagnosis because it still goes through `rtk`.
5. `rtk run` uses shell execution semantics; prefer RTK-native commands first, then `rtk proxy` when quoting is simpler.
6. Do not use `rtk read --line` or `--line-count`; current `rtk read` supports `--max-lines`, `--tail-lines`, and `--line-numbers`.
7. `rtk powershell ...` is valid when PowerShell is the intended program; `rtk proxy powershell ...` is also acceptable for diagnosis.
8. On this Windows environment, use `powershell`, not `pwsh`, unless `rtk proxy where.exe pwsh` confirms PowerShell 7 is installed.

## Correct Windows Recipes

Use these positive patterns for recurring Windows command scenarios:

1. PowerShell on this machine: use `rtk powershell ...` by default; only use `pwsh` after `rtk proxy where.exe pwsh` confirms it exists.
2. Inline PowerShell containing `$`, `$env:...`, or `$_`: use outer single quotes around the PowerShell script, or escape each `$` as `` `$ `` when outer double quotes are unavoidable.
3. PowerShell regex alternation: quote the regex inside the PowerShell script, for example `-match "node|vite|wps"`.
4. Python scripts: run with `rtk python script.py`; do not use PowerShell as the interpreter.
5. Node scripts: run with `rtk node script.mjs` or the project package wrapper, not through PowerShell unless shell features are needed.
6. PowerShell scripts: run with `rtk powershell -NoProfile -ExecutionPolicy Bypass -File .\script.ps1`.
7. Builds with post-build installers: use the project wrapper through `rtk`, then verify generated artifact timestamps and install directories if the command takes longer than expected.
8. Command diagnosis: classify failures as RTK resolution, PowerShell parsing, target-program error, or timeout before retrying.

## High-Frequency Command Recipes

Use this section before inventing command syntax.

### Git

Prefer RTK-native Git wrappers:

```bash
rtk git status --short
rtk git diff --stat
rtk git diff -- path/to/file
rtk git log -1 --oneline
rtk git add path/to/file
rtk git commit -m "Message"
rtk git push origin main
rtk git pull
rtk git branch --show-current
```

Notes:

1. `rtk git add`, `rtk git commit`, and `rtk git push` intentionally produce compact `ok` output.
2. Use `rtk proxy git ...` only when exact raw Git output is needed.
3. Use `rtk git diff -- path` for repository diffs; `rtk diff` is for 
... (truncated)
```
</details></tool-use>

---
name: windows-powershell
description: Use when composing or running Windows PowerShell commands from Codex, especially through `rtk powershell`, `powershell -Command`, `powershell -File`, commands with `$_`, pipes, quotes, paths with spaces, environment variables, or scripts. Prevents repeated quoting, escaping, and script invocation mistakes on Windows.
---

# Windows PowerShell

Use this skill before running non-trivial PowerShell commands on Windows. Keep updates concise: add only correct reusable command forms and decision rules, not failing commands or incident notes.

## Command Review

Before executing, check:

- Entry mode: `rtk powershell -Command` for inline script, `rtk powershell -File` only for `.ps1`.
- Write scope: target files, install directories, generated artifacts, processes.
- Fragile tokens: `$_`, `$env:...`, `|`, `>`, `<`, `&`, `;`, quotes, paths with spaces.
- Whether a small read-only probe should run before a write/copy/remove/build/install command.

## Entry Rules

- Run PowerShell scripts as:

```powershell
rtk powershell -NoProfile -ExecutionPolicy Bypass -File ".\scripts\task.ps1"
```

- Run `.py`, `.js`, `.mjs`, `.bat`, or `.cmd` with their own executable, not `powershell -File`.
- Use `rtk <program> ...` directly when PowerShell is unnecessary, for example `rtk git status`, `rtk npm test`, `rtk node script.mjs`.

## Quoting Rules

Prefer outer single quotes when inline PowerShell contains `$`, `$env:...`, or `$_`:

```powershell
rtk powershell -NoProfile -Command 'Get-Process | Where-Object { $_.ProcessName -match "node|vite|wps" } | Select-Object Id,ProcessName'
```

```powershell
rtk powershell -NoProfile -Command '$p = Join-Path $env:APPDATA "kingsoft\wps"; Get-ChildItem -LiteralPath $p'
```

When outer double quotes are required by the caller, escape every PowerShell `$` as backtick-dollar:

```powershell
rtk powershell -NoProfile -Command "`$target = Join-Path `$env:APPDATA 'kingsoft\wps\jsaddons'; Get-ChildItem -LiteralPath `$target -Directory | Select-Object Name,LastWriteTime"
```

For `$_` with outer double quotes, escape `$_` and quote regex alternation inside the script:

```powershell
rtk powershell -NoProfile -Command "Get-Process | Where-Object { `$_.ProcessName -match 'node|vite|wps|powershell' } | Select-Object Id,ProcessName,StartTime"
```

For nested quotes in WMI/CIM filters, assign the filter to a variable first:

```powershell
rtk powershell -NoProfile -Command "`$filter = 'Name = ''powershell.exe'''; Get-CimInstance Win32_Process -Filter `$filter | Select-Object ProcessId,CommandLine"
```

## Paths And Files

- Use `-LiteralPath` for exact paths, especially paths with spaces, brackets, wildcards, or non-ASCII characters.
- Quote all Windows paths.
- Prefer `Join-Path` for paths built from `$env:APPDATA`, `$HOME`, or workspace variables.

Read a file:

```powershell
rtk powershell -NoProfile -Command 'Get-Content -LiteralPath "C:\path\file.txt"'
```

List selected files:

```powershell
rtk powershell -NoProfile -Command 'Get-ChildItem -LiteralPath "C:\path" -Force | Select-Object Name,LastWriteTime,Length'
```

Inspect generated or installed artifacts under `%APPDATA%`:

```powershell
rtk powershell -NoProfile -Command "`$asset = Join-Path `$env:APPDATA 'kingsoft\wps\jsaddons\wps-ai_1.0.4\assets'; Get-ChildItem -LiteralPath `$asset -Filter 'taskpane-*.js' | Select-Object Name,LastWriteTime,Length"
```

## Write Commands

Before `Copy-Item`, `Remove-Item`, `Move-Item`, `Set-Content`, process kills, installers, or builds that copy output:

- Resolve or print the target path first.
- Confirm the target is inside the intended workspace or named install directory.
- Prefer `-LiteralPath` for removal/move targets.
- Do not compose destructive filesystem commands across shells.
- For recursive removal, verify the resolved absolute path before running the removal.

## Failure Recovery

When a PowerShell command fails:

- Classify the failure as RTK resolution, PowerShell parsing, command semantics, or target-program error.
- If `rtk` ran and PowerShell reported a parser error, fix quoting or invocation mode; do not fall back to raw shell.
- For `$_` problems, use outer single quotes or escape `$` under outer double quotes.
- After finding a reusable correct form, update this skill before the final response.
```
</details></tool-use>

  +function normalizeLanguage(className = "") {

  +  return match?.[1] || "text";
  +}
  +
  +function CodeBlock({ className, code }) {
  +  const language = useMemo(() => normalizeLanguage(className), [className]);
  +  const [html, setHtml] = useState("");
  +  const [copied, setCopied] = useState(false);
  +
  +  useEffect(() => {
  +    let active = true;
  +
  +    async function renderHighlightedCode() {
  +      try {
  +        const { codeToHtml } = await import("shiki");
  +        const nextHtml = await codeToHtml(code, {
  +          lang: language,
  +          theme: "github-light"
  +        });
  +
  +        if (active) {
  +          setHtml(nextHtml);
  +        }
  +      } catch {
  +        if (active) {
  +          setHtml("");
  +        }
  +      }
  +    }
  +
  +    void renderHighlightedCode();
  +
  +    return () => {
  +      active = false;
  +    };
  +  }, [code, language]);
  +
  +  async function copyCode() {
  +    try {
  +      await navigator.clipboard?.writeText(code);
  +      setCopied(true);
  +      window.setTimeout(() => setCopied(false), 1200);
  +    } catch {
  +      setCopied(false);
  +    }
  +  }
  +
  +  return (
  +    <div className="chat-code-block">
  +      <div className="chat-code-toolbar">
  +        <span>{language}</span>
  +        <button type="button" onClick={copyCode}>
  +          {copied ? "Copied" : "Copy"}
  +        </button>
  +      </div>
  +      <div
  +        className="chat-code-shiki"
  +        dangerouslySetInnerHTML={{
  +          __html:
  +            html ||
  +            `<pre><code>${code
  +              .replace(/&/g, "&amp;")
  +              .replace(/</g, "&lt;")
  +              .replace(/>/g, "&gt;")}</code></pre>`
  +        }}
  +      />
  +    </div>
  +  );
  +}

  9 тФВ function flattenText(node) {
 10 тФВ   if (typeof node === "string" || typeof node === "number") {
 11 тФВ     return String(node);
 12 тФВ   }
 13 тФВ 
 14 тФВ   if (Array.isArray(node)) {
 15 тФВ     return node.map((item) => flattenText(item)).join("");
 16 тФВ   }
 17 тФВ

 19 тФВ     return flattenText(node.props?.children);
 20 тФВ   }
 21 тФВ 
 22 тФВ   return "";
 23 тФВ }
 24 тФВ 
 25 тФВ function normalizeLanguage(className = "") {
 26 тФВ   const match = String(className).match(/\blanguage-([\w-]+)/);
 27 тФВ   return match?.[1] || "text";
 28 тФВ }
 29 тФВ 
 30 тФВ function CodeBlock({ className, code }) {
 31 тФВ   const language = useMemo(() => normalizeLanguage(className), [className]);
 32 тФВ   const [html, setHtml] = useState("");
 33 тФВ   const [copied, setCopied] = useState(false);
 34 тФВ

 36 тФВ     let active = true;
 37 тФВ

 39 тФВ       try {
 40 тФВ         const { codeToHtml } = await import("shiki");
 41 тФВ         const nextHtml = await codeToHtml(code, {
 42 тФВ           lang: language,
 43 тФВ           theme: "github-light"
 44 тФВ         });
 45 тФВ 
 46 тФВ         if (active) {
 47 тФВ           setHtml(nextHtml);
 48 тФВ         }
 49 тФВ       } catch {
 50 тФВ         if (active) {
 51 тФВ           setHtml("");
 52 тФВ         }
 53 тФВ       }

 55 тФВ 
 56 тФВ     void renderHighlightedCode();
 57 тФВ

 61 тФВ   }, [code, language]);
 62 тФВ 
 63 тФВ   async function copyCode() {
 64 тФВ     try {

 73 тФВ   return (

.bubble-markdown li + li {
  margin-top: 0.24em;

  overflow-wrap: anywhere;

.bubble-markdown li > p,

  text-indent: 0;

  margin-left: 0;

  border-left: 3px solid rgba(0, 113, 227, 0.24);
  color: #5f6b7a;

.bubble-markdown blockquote ol {
  margin-left: 0;
}

.bubble-markdown code {
  padding: 0.16em 0.42em;
  border-radius: 7px;
  border: 1px solid rgba(17, 17, 17, 0.08);
  background: rgba(17, 17, 17, 0.05);
  font-family: "Cascadia Code", Consolas, monospace;

}

  overflow-x: auto;

  border: 1px solid rgba(17, 17, 17, 0.08);
  border-radius: 16px;
  background: #f6f8fb;

.bubble-markdown pre code {
  padding: 0;
  border: 0;
  background: transparent;
  font-size: 12.5px;
  line-height: 1.65;
}

.chat-code-block {
  margin: 0 0 0.9em;
  overflow: hidden;
  border: 1px solid rgba(17, 17, 17, 0.08);
  border-radius: 16px;
  background: #f6f8fb;
  box-shadow: inset 0 1px 0 rgba(255, 255, 255, 0.7);
}

.chat-code-toolbar {
  min-height: 34px;
  padding: 0 10px 0 12px;
  display: flex;
  align-items: center;
  justify-content: space-between;
  gap: 10px;
  border-bottom: 1px solid rgba(17, 17, 17, 0.07);
  color: rgba(29, 29, 31, 0.58);
  font-size: 12px;
}

.chat-code-toolbar button {
  min-height: 24px;
  padding: 0 8px;
  border: 1px solid rgba(0, 113, 227, 0.12);
  border-radius: 999px;
  color: var(--accent-strong);

}

}

.chat-code-shiki pre,

  margin: 0;

  line-height: 1.65;
}

.bubble-markdown hr {
  height: 1px;
  border: 0;
  background: rgba(17, 17, 17, 0.1);
}

 function normalizeLanguage(className = "") {
   const match = String(className).match(/\blanguage-([\w-]+)/);
   return match?.[1] || "text";
 }
 
+function highlightCode(code, language) {
+  try {
+    if (language && language !== "text" && hljs.getLanguage(language)) {
+      return hljs.highlight(code, {
+        ignoreIllegals: true,
+        language
+      }).value;
+    }
+
+    return hljs.highlightAuto(code).value;
+  } catch {
+    return hljs.highlight(code, {
+      ignoreIllegals: true,
+      language: "plaintext"
+    }).value;
+  }
+}
+
 function CodeBlock({ className, code }) {
   const language = useMemo(() => normalizeLanguage(className), [className]);
-  const [html, setHtml] = useState("");

+import json from "highlight.js/lib/languages/json";
+import markdown from "highlight.js/lib/languages/markdown";
+import plaintext from "highlight.js/lib/languages/plaintext";
+import powershell from "highlight.js/lib/languages/powershell";
+import python from "highlight.js/lib/languages/python";
+import rust from "highlight.js/lib/languages/rust";
+import sql from "highlight.js/lib/languages/sql";
+import typescript from "highlight.js/lib/languages/typescript";
+import xml from "highlight.js/lib/languages/xml";
+import yaml from "highlight.js/lib/languages/yaml";
+
+const REGISTERED_LANGUAGES = {
+  bash,
+  csharp,
+  css,
+  diff,
+  dos,
+  go,
+  java,
+  javascript,
+  json,
+  markdown,
+  plaintext,
+  powershell,
+  python,
+  rust,
+  sql,
+  typescript,
+  xml,
+  yaml
+};
+
+for (const [name, language] of Object.entries(REGISTERED_LANGUAGES)) {
+  hljs.registerLanguage(name, language);
+}
+
+export function normalizeCodeLanguage(language) {
+  const normalized = String(language || "").trim().toLowerCase();
+  if (!normalized) {
+    return "plaintext";
+  }
+
+  const aliases = {
+    c: "csharp",
+    "c++": "csharp",
+    bash: "bash",
+    cjs: "javascript",
+    cmd: "dos",
+    cs: "csharp",
+    html: "xml",
+    js: "javascript",
+    jsx: "javascript",
+    md: "markdown",
+    mjs: "javascript",
+    ps1: "powershell",
+    py: "python",
+    shell: "bash",
+    sh: "bash",
+    text: "plaintext",
+    ts: "typescript",
+    tsx: "typescript",
+    xml: "xml",
+    yml: "yaml"
+  };
+
+  return aliases[normalized] || normalized;
+}
+
+export default hljs;
```
</details></tool-use>

-import { Children, isValidElement } from "react";
+import { Children, isValidElement, useMemo, useState } from "react";
+import hljs, { normalizeCodeLanguage } from "./codeHighlighter";
 import ReactMarkdown from "react-markdown";
-import rehypeHighlight from "rehype-highlight";
 import rehypeKatex from "rehype-katex";
 import rehypeRaw from "rehype-raw";
 import remarkGfm from "remark-gfm";
@@ -24,6 +24,62 @@ function flattenText(node) {
   return "";
 }
 
+function normalizeLanguage(className = "") {
+  const match = String(className).match(/\blanguage-([\w-]+)/);
+  return normalizeCodeLanguage(match?.[1]);
+}
+
+function highlightCode(code, language) {
+  try {
+    if (language && language !== "text" && hljs.getLanguage(language)) {
+      return hljs.highlight(code, {
+        ignoreIllegals: true,
+        language
+      }).value;
+    }
+
+    return hljs.highlightAuto(code).value;
+  } catch {
+    return hljs.highlight(code, {
+      ignoreIllegals: true,
+      language: "plaintext"
+    }).value;
+  }
+}
+
+function CodeBlock({ className, code }) {
+  const language = useMemo(() => normalizeLanguage(className), [className]);
+  const html = useMemo(() => highlightCode(code, language), [code, language]);
+  const [copied, setCopied] = useState(false);
+
+  async function copyCode() {
+    try {
+      await navigator.clipboard?.writeText(code);
+      setCopied(true);
+      window.setTimeout(() => setCopied(false), 1200);
+    } catch {
+      setCopied(false);
+    }
+  }
+
+  return (
+    <div className="chat-code-block">
+      <div className="chat-code-toolbar">
+        <span>{language}</span>
+        <button type="button" onClick={copyCode}>
+          {copied ? "Copied" : "Copy"}
+        </button>
+      </div>
+      <pre className="chat-code-content">
+        <code
+          className={`hljs language-${language}`}
+          dangerouslySetInnerHTML={{ __html: html }}
+        />
+      </pre>
+    </div>
+  );
+}
+
 export default function StreamingMarkdown({ content, streaming }) {
   const markdown = String(content ?? "");
 
@@ -35,18 +91,36 @@ export default function StreamingMarkdown({ content, streaming }) {

         components={{
           a(props) {
             return <a {...props} target="_blank" rel="noreferrer" />;
           },
+          pre({ children }) {
+            const child = Children.toArray(children).find(isValidElement);
+            if (child?.props?.["data-code-block"]) {
+              return (
+                <CodeBlock
+                  className={child.props.className || ""}
+                  code={child.props["data-code-value"] || ""}
+                />
+              );
+            }
+
+            return <pre>{children}</pre>;
+          },

             const isBlock = /\blanguage-/.test(className || "") || value.includes("\n");
 
             if (isBlock) {
               return (
-                <code className={className} {...props}>

+                  data-code-value={value.replace(/\n$/, "")}
+                  {...props}
+                >
                   {value.replace(/\n$/, "")}
                 </code>
               );

```diff
@@
 rtk git diff --stat
 rtk git diff -- path/to/file
+rtk proxy git diff -- path/to/file path/to/other-file
 rtk git log -1 --oneline
@@
 1. `rtk git add`, `rtk git commit`, and `rtk git push` intentionally produce compact `ok` output.
 2. Use `rtk proxy git ...` only when exact raw Git output is needed.
 3. Use `rtk git diff -- path` for repository diffs; `rtk diff` is for comparing files or filtering unified diff stdin.
+4. For multiple pathspecs or when native diff argument parsing is ambiguous, use `rtk proxy git diff -- <paths...>`.
```
</details></tool-use>

 3 тФВ **Usage**: Token-optimized CLI proxy for shell commands.

 5 тФВ ## Rule

12 тФВ rtk git status

20 тФВ ```bash
21 тФВ rtk gain            # Token savings analytics
22 тФВ rtk gain --history  # Recent command savings history
23 тФВ rtk proxy <cmd>     # Run raw command without filtering
24 тФВ ```
25 тФВ 
26 тФВ ## Verification
27 тФВ 
28 тФВ ```bash
29 тФВ rtk --version
30 тФВ rtk gain
31 тФВ which rtk
32 тФВ ```
```
</details></tool-use>

<tool-use data-tool-type="shell" data-tool-name="shell_command"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **shell_command** `rtk read -n --max-lines 260 C:\Users\10297441.WIN-9DOP5T7GHM7\.codex\skills\rtk-usage\SKILL.md`</summary>

  1 тФВ ---
  2 тФВ name: rtk-usage
  3 тФВ description: Use when working with `rtk` on Windows or Codex, especially for command selection, validating `rtk` behavior, fixing `rtk` failures, diagnosing `No hook installed`, or avoiding incorrect fallback to raw shell commands. Trigger for `rtk` installation, upgrade, init, hook, rewrite, proxy, env, json, verify, gain, and command-usage troubleshooting.
  4 тФВ ---
  5 тФВ 
  6 тФВ # RTK Usage
  7 тФВ 
  8 тФВ Use this skill whenever a task depends on `rtk` working correctly.
  9 тФВ 
 10 тФВ ## Core Rule
 11 тФВ 
 12 тФВ 1. If an `rtk` command fails, do not fall back to the equivalent raw shell command.
 13 тФВ 2. Diagnose and fix the `rtk` issue first.
 14 тФВ 3. Only continue the main task after `rtk` is working again.
 15 тФВ 4. Prefer RTK-native subcommands over `rtk run` / `rtk proxy` when a native subcommand exists.
 16 тФВ 5. On Windows, quote carefully in the outer shell; many apparent RTK failures are PowerShell quoting errors.
 17 тФВ 6. If a non-interactive command can be run through `rtk`, run it through `rtk`.
 18 тФВ 7. Raw shell is allowed only when exact byte-for-byte output matters, the command is tiny/trivial, the command is interactive, or `rtk` would change semantics.
 19 тФВ 8. When updating this skill, add only correct reusable RTK command forms and decision rules.
 20 тФВ 
 21 тФВ ## Windows Baseline Checks
 22 тФВ 
 23 тФВ 1. Run `rtk --version`.
 24 тФВ 2. Run `rtk --help`.
 25 тФВ 3. Run `rtk gain`.
 26 тФВ 4. Run `rtk init -g --show` for Claude hook status.
 27 тФВ 5. Run `rtk init -g --codex --show` for Codex global status.
 28 тФВ 6. Run `rtk verify`.
 29 тФВ 7. Run `rtk proxy where.exe rtk` to confirm the active binary.
 30 тФВ 8. On Windows release installs, prefer the official GitHub asset `rtk-x86_64-pc-windows-msvc.zip`.
 31 тФВ 
 32 тФВ Known-good Windows baseline observed for `rtk 0.37.2`:
 33 тФВ 
 34 тФВ ```bash
 35 тФВ rtk --version
 36 тФВ rtk --help
 37 тФВ rtk gain
 38 тФВ rtk verify
 39 тФВ rtk init -g --show
 40 тФВ rtk init -g --codex --show
 41 тФВ rtk proxy where.exe rtk
 42 тФВ ```
 43 тФВ 
 44 тФВ ## Correct Command Patterns
 45 тФВ 
 46 тФВ Use these forms exactly:
 47 тФВ 
 48 тФВ ```bash
 49 тФВ rtk env --filter PATH
 50 тФВ rtk json package.json
 51 тФВ rtk rewrite "git status --short"
 52 тФВ rtk proxy git status --short
 53 тФВ rtk proxy powershell -NoProfile -Command "Get-Content package.json -TotalCount 20"
 54 тФВ rtk powershell -NoProfile -Command "Get-Content package.json -TotalCount 20"
 55 тФВ rtk run "git branch --show-current"
 56 тФВ rtk run -c "git status --short"
 57 тФВ rtk verify
 58 тФВ rtk help hook
 59 тФВ rtk hook check git status --short
 60 тФВ ```
 61 тФВ 
 62 тФВ Rules:
 63 тФВ 
 64 тФВ 1. `rtk env` uses `--filter`; do not pass variable names as positional arguments.
 65 тФВ 2. `rtk json` takes a JSON file path; do not pass inline JSON text.
 66 тФВ 3. `rtk rewrite` can exit non-zero even after printing a valid rewrite in some Windows builds; judge by printed output and `rtk rewrite --help`, not by code alone.
 67 тФВ 4. `rtk proxy` is acceptable for diagnosis because it still goes through `rtk`.
 68 тФВ 5. `rtk run` uses shell execution semantics; prefer RTK-native commands first, then `rtk proxy` when quoting is simpler.
 69 тФВ 6. Do not use `rtk read --line` or `--line-count`; current `rtk read` supports `--max-lines`, `--tail-lines`, and `--line-numbers`.
 70 тФВ 7. `rtk powershell ...` is valid when PowerShell is the intended program; `rtk proxy powershell ...` is also acceptable for diagnosis.
 71 тФВ 8. On this Windows environment, use `powershell`, not `pwsh`, unless `rtk proxy where.exe pwsh` confirms PowerShell 7 is installed.
 72 тФВ 
 73 тФВ ## Correct Windows Recipes
 74 тФВ 
 75 тФВ Use these positive patterns for recurring Windows command scenarios:
 76 тФВ 
 77 тФВ 1. PowerShell on this machine: use `rtk powershell ...` by default; only use `pwsh` after `rtk proxy where.exe pwsh` confirms it exists.
 78 тФВ 2. Inline PowerShell containing `$`, `$env:...`, or `$_`: use outer single quotes around the PowerShell script, or escape each `$` as `` `$ `` when outer double quotes are unavoidable.
 79 тФВ 3. PowerShell regex alternation: quote the regex inside the PowerShell script, for example `-match "node|vite|wps"`.
 80 тФВ 4. Python scripts: run with `rtk python script.py`; do not use PowerShell as the interpreter.
 81 тФВ 5. Node scripts: run with `rtk node script.mjs` or the project package wrapper, not through PowerShell unless shell features are needed.
 82 тФВ 6. PowerShell scripts: run with `rtk powershell -NoProfile -ExecutionPolicy Bypass -File .\script.ps1`.
 83 тФВ 7. Builds with post-build installers: use the project wrapper through `rtk`, then verify generated artifact timestamps and install directories if the command takes longer than expected.
 84 тФВ 8. Command diagnosis: classify failures as RTK resolution, Pow
... (truncated)
```
</details></tool-use>

<tool-use data-tool-type="shell" data-tool-name="shell_command"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **shell_command** `rtk read -n --max-lines 220 C:\Users\10297441.WIN-9DOP5T7GHM7\.codex\skills\windows-powershell\SKILL.md`</summary>
```
Exit code: 0
Wall time: 0.6 seconds
Output:
  1 тФВ ---
  2 тФВ name: windows-powershell
  3 тФВ description: Use when composing or running Windows PowerShell commands from Codex, especially through `rtk powershell`, `powershell -Command`, `powershell -File`, commands with `$_`, pipes, quotes, paths with spaces, environment variables, or scripts. Prevents repeated quoting, escaping, and script invocation mistakes on Windows.
  4 тФВ ---
  5 тФВ 
  6 тФВ # Windows PowerShell
  7 тФВ 
  8 тФВ Use this skill before running non-trivial PowerShell commands on Windows. Keep updates concise: add only correct reusable command forms and decision rules, not failing commands or incident notes.
  9 тФВ 
 10 тФВ ## Command Review
 11 тФВ 
 12 тФВ Before executing, check:
 13 тФВ 
 14 тФВ - Entry mode: `rtk powershell -Command` for inline script, `rtk powershell -File` only for `.ps1`.
 15 тФВ - Write scope: target files, install directories, generated artifacts, processes.
 16 тФВ - Fragile tokens: `$_`, `$env:...`, `|`, `>`, `<`, `&`, `;`, quotes, paths with spaces.
 17 тФВ - Whether a small read-only probe should run before a write/copy/remove/build/install command.
 18 тФВ 
 19 тФВ ## Entry Rules
 20 тФВ 
 21 тФВ - Run PowerShell scripts as:
 22 тФВ 
 23 тФВ ```powershell
 24 тФВ rtk powershell -NoProfile -ExecutionPolicy Bypass -File ".\scripts\task.ps1"
 25 тФВ ```
 26 тФВ 
 27 тФВ - Run `.py`, `.js`, `.mjs`, `.bat`, or `.cmd` with their own executable, not `powershell -File`.
 28 тФВ - Use `rtk <program> ...` directly when PowerShell is unnecessary, for example `rtk git status`, `rtk npm test`, `rtk node script.mjs`.
 29 тФВ 
 30 тФВ ## Quoting Rules
 31 тФВ 
 32 тФВ Prefer outer single quotes when inline PowerShell contains `$`, `$env:...`, or `$_`:
 33 тФВ 
 34 тФВ ```powershell
 35 тФВ rtk powershell -NoProfile -Command 'Get-Process | Where-Object { $_.ProcessName -match "node|vite|wps" } | Select-Object Id,ProcessName'
 36 тФВ ```
 37 тФВ 
 38 тФВ ```powershell
 39 тФВ rtk powershell -NoProfile -Command '$p = Join-Path $env:APPDATA "kingsoft\wps"; Get-ChildItem -LiteralPath $p'
 40 тФВ ```
 41 тФВ 
 42 тФВ When outer double quotes are required by the caller, escape every PowerShell `$` as backtick-dollar:
 43 тФВ 
 44 тФВ ```powershell
 45 тФВ rtk powershell -NoProfile -Command "`$target = Join-Path `$env:APPDATA 'kingsoft\wps\jsaddons'; Get-ChildItem -LiteralPath `$target -Directory | Select-Object Name,LastWriteTime"
 46 тФВ ```
 47 тФВ 
 48 тФВ For `$_` with outer double quotes, escape `$_` and quote regex alternation inside the script:
 49 тФВ 
 50 тФВ ```powershell
 51 тФВ rtk powershell -NoProfile -Command "Get-Process | Where-Object { `$_.ProcessName -match 'node|vite|wps|powershell' } | Select-Object Id,ProcessName,StartTime"
 52 тФВ ```
 53 тФВ 
 54 тФВ For nested quotes in WMI/CIM filters, assign the filter to a variable first:
 55 тФВ 
 56 тФВ ```powershell
 57 тФВ rtk powershell -NoProfile -Command "`$filter = 'Name = ''powershell.exe'''; Get-CimInstance Win32_Process -Filter `$filter | Select-Object ProcessId,CommandLine"
 58 тФВ ```
 59 тФВ 
 60 тФВ ## Paths And Files
 61 тФВ 
 62 тФВ - Use `-LiteralPath` for exact paths, especially paths with spaces, brackets, wildcards, or non-ASCII characters.
 63 тФВ - Quote all Windows paths.
 64 тФВ - Prefer `Join-Path` for paths built from `$env:APPDATA`, `$HOME`, or workspace variables.
 65 тФВ 
 66 тФВ Read a file:
 67 тФВ 
 68 тФВ ```powershell
 69 тФВ rtk powershell -NoProfile -Command 'Get-Content -LiteralPath "C:\path\file.txt"'
 70 тФВ ```
 71 тФВ 
 72 тФВ List selected files:
 73 тФВ 
 74 тФВ ```powershell
 75 тФВ rtk powershell -NoProfile -Command 'Get-ChildItem -LiteralPath "C:\path" -Force | Select-Object Name,LastWriteTime,Length'
 76 тФВ ```
 77 тФВ 
 78 тФВ Inspect generated or installed artifacts under `%APPDATA%`:
 79 тФВ 
 80 тФВ ```powershell
 81 тФВ rtk powershell -NoProfile -Command "`$asset = Join-Path `$env:APPDATA 'kingsoft\wps\jsaddons\wps-ai_1.0.4\assets'; Get-ChildItem -LiteralPath `$asset -Filter 'taskpane-*.js' | Select-Object Name,LastWriteTime,Length"
 82 тФВ ```
 83 тФВ 
 84 тФВ For timestamp checks where the target directory is already specific, list and sort the target directly instead of embedding complex regex filters:
 85 тФВ 
 86 тФВ ```powershell
 87 тФВ rtk powershell -NoProfile -Command '$target = Join-Path $env:APPDATA "kingsoft\wps\jsaddons\wps-ai_1.0.4"; Get-ChildItem -LiteralPath $target -Recurse -File | Sort-Object LastWriteTime -Descending | Select-Object -First 12 FullName,LastWriteTime,Length'
 88 тФВ ```
 89 тФВ 
 90 тФВ ## Write Commands
 91 тФВ 
 92 тФВ Before `Copy-Item`, `Remove-Item`, `Move-Item`, `Set-Content`, process kills, installers, or builds that copy output:
 93 тФВ 
 94 тФВ - Resolve or print the target path first.
 95 тФВ - Confirm the target is inside the intended workspace or named install directory.
 96 тФВ - Prefer `-LiteralPath` for removal/move targets.
 97 тФВ - Do not compose destructi
... (truncated)
```
</details></tool-use>

15 тФВ public class WindowCapture {
16 тФВ   [StructLayout(LayoutKind.Sequential)]
17 тФВ   public struct RECT {
18 тФВ     public int Left;

20 тФВ     public int Right;

30 тФВ "@
31 тФВ 
32 тФВ $process = if ($ProcessId -gt 0) {

34 тФВ     Where-Object { $_.MainWindowHandle -ne 0 } |
35 тФВ     Select-Object -First 1

56 тФВ try {

  function applyModel(modelId) {
    updateSetting("model", modelId);
    setShowModelLab(false);
    setModelSearch("");
  }

  async function handleGenerate() {
    const trimmedPrompt = prompt.trim();
    if (!settings.apiKey.trim()) {
      setError("Enter the API key first.");
      openSettings();
      return;
    }

    if (!settings.model.trim()) {
      setError("Enter the model name first.");
      openSettings();
      return;
    }

    if (!trimmedPrompt) {
      return;
    }

    setError("");
    setIsGenerating(true);
    pushDebugLog("info", "ui", "User submitted a generation request.", {
      promptLength: trimmedPrompt.length
    });

    let latestSelectionText = selectionText;
    if (settings.useSelectionAsContext) {
      latestSelectionText = readSelectionText();
      setSelectionText(latestSelectionText);
    }

    const controller = new AbortController();
    const userMessageId = createMessageId();
    const assistantMessageId = createMessageId();
    const sink = createWpsMarkdownSink({
      replaceSelection: settings.replaceSelection
    });

    abortRef.current = controller;
    liveSinkRef.current = {
      messageId: assistantMessageId,
      sink
    };
    shouldStickToBottomRef.current = true;

    setMessages((current) => [
      ...current,
      { id: userMessageId, role: "user", content: trimmedPrompt },
      { id: assistantMessageId, role: "assistant", content: "", streaming: true }
    ]);

    try {
      let aggregate = "";

      for await (const chunk of streamCompletion({
        settings,
        messages: buildMessages({
          prompt: trimmedPrompt,
          selectionText: latestSelectionText,
          settings,
          history: latestMessagesRef.current
        }),
        signal: controller.signal
      })) {
        aggregate += chunk;
        queueAssistantChunk(assistantMessageId, chunk);
        sink.write(chunk);
      }

      sink.finish();
      pushDebugLog("info", "wps", "WPS sink finished appending streamed content.", {
        chars: aggregate.length
      });
      flushBufferedAssistantMessage(assistantMessageId);
      setMessages((current) =>
        current.map((message) =>
          message.id === assistantMessageId
            ? { ...message, content: aggregate, streaming: false }
            : message
        )
      );
      setPrompt("");
    } catch (caught) {
      const aborted =
        caught instanceof DOMException && caught.name === "AbortError";

      flushBufferedAssistantMessage(assistantMessageId);

      if (aborted) {
        sink.cancel();
        pushDebugLog("warn", "ui", "Generation aborted by the user.");
        setMessages((current) =>
          current.map((message) =>
            message.id === assistantMessageId
              ? { ...message, streaming: false }
              : message
          )
        );
      } else {
        const message = caught instanceof Error ? caught.message : "Generation failed.";
        setError(message);
        pushDebugLog("error", "ui", "Generation ended with an error.", {
          error: message
        });
        setMessages((current) =>
          current.map((entry) =>
            entry.id === assistantMessageId
              ? {
                  ...entry,
                  content: entry.content || "Request failed.",
                  streaming: false,
                  error: true
                }
              : entry
          )
        );
```
</details></tool-use>

<tool-use data-tool-type="write" data-tool-name="apply_patch"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **apply_patch**</summary>
**Add: `scripts/inspect-wps-ui.ps1`**

```diff
+param(
+  [string]$TitleLike = "WPS цЦЗхнЧ",
+  [int]$MaxDepth = 6
+)
+
+$ErrorActionPreference = "Stop"
+
+Add-Type -AssemblyName UIAutomationClient
+Add-Type -AssemblyName UIAutomationTypes
+
+$process = Get-Process -Name wps -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue |
+  Where-Object { $_.MainWindowHandle -ne 0 -and $_.MainWindowTitle -like "*$TitleLike*" } |
+  Sort-Object StartTime -Descending |
+  Select-Object -First 1
+
+if (-not $process) {
+  throw "No WPS window matched $TitleLike."
+}
+
+function Format-Rect($rect) {
+  if ($rect.IsEmpty) {
+    return ""
+  }
+
+  return "{0},{1},{2},{3}" -f [int]$rect.X, [int]$rect.Y, [int]$rect.Width, [int]$rect.Height
+}
+
+function Walk-Element($element, $depth) {
+  if ($null -eq $element -or $depth -gt $MaxDepth) {
+    return
+  }
+
+  $prefix = "  " * $depth
+  $controlType = $element.Current.ControlType.ProgrammaticName -replace "^ControlType\.", ""
+  $name = ($element.Current.Name -replace "\s+", " ").Trim()
+  $automationId = ($element.Current.AutomationId -replace "\s+", " ").Trim()
+  $className = ($element.Current.ClassName -replace "\s+", " ").Trim()
+  $rect = Format-Rect $element.Current.BoundingRectangle
+
+  Write-Output ("{0}{1} name=[{2}] id=[{3}] class=[{4}] offscreen=[{5}] rect=[{6}]" -f $prefix, $controlType, $name, $automationId, $className, $element.Current.IsOffscreen, $rect)
+
+  $children = $element.FindAll(
+    [System.Windows.Automation.TreeScope]::Children,
+    [System.Windows.Automation.Condition]::TrueCondition
+  )
+
+  foreach ($child in $children) {
+    Walk-Element $child ($depth + 1)
+  }
+}
+
+$root = [System.Windows.Automation.AutomationElement]::FromHandle($process.MainWindowHandle)
+Write-Output ("PROCESS={0} TITLE={1}" -f $process.Id, $process.MainWindowTitle)
+Walk-Element $root 0
```
</details></tool-use>

  const [debugLogs, setDebugLogs] = useState(() => readDebugLogs());
  const [copyLogStatus, setCopyLogStatus] = useState("");

  const abortRef = useRef(null);
  const benchmarkAbortRef = useRef(null);
  const promptRef = useRef(null);
  const threadRef = useRef(null);
  const shouldStickToBottomRef = useRef(true);
  const liveSinkRef = useRef(null);
  const activeDocumentRef = useRef(readDocumentInfo());
  const isGeneratingRef = useRef(false);
  const latestMessagesRef = useRef([]);
  const messageBufferRef = useRef({
    chunk: "",
    messageId: "",
    timerId: null
  });
  const sessionLoadRef = useRef({
    busy: false,
    token: 0
  });
  const sessionSaveTimerRef = useRef(null);

  useEffect(() => {
    saveStoredValue(SETTINGS_KEY, settings);
  }, [settings]);

  useEffect(() => {
    saveStoredValue(PROMPT_KEY, prompt);
  }, [prompt]);

  useEffect(() => {
    saveStoredValue(MODEL_CATALOG_KEY, modelCatalog);
  }, [modelCatalog]);

  useEffect(() => {
    return subscribeDebugLogs(setDebugLogs);
  }, []);

  useEffect(() => {
    latestMessagesRef.current = messages;
  }, [messages]);

  useEffect(() => {
    isGeneratingRef.current = isGenerating;
  }, [isGenerating]);

  useEffect(() => {
    if (hasWpsDocument()) {
      setSelectionText(readSelectionText());
    }
  }, []);

  useEffect(() => {
    if (!shouldRunWpsE2E()) {
      return;
    }

    const e2eKey = "wps_ai_e2e_rich_format_done";
    if (window.sessionStorage.getItem(e2eKey) === "1") {
      return;
    }

    const timerId = window.setTimeout(() => {
      try {
        const sink = createWpsMarkdownSink({ replaceSelection: false });
        sink.write(E2E_RICH_FORMAT_SAMPLE);
        sink.finish();
        window.sessionStorage.setItem(e2eKey, "1");
      } catch (e) {
        console.error("WPS E2E table test failed:", e);
      }
    }, 1200);

    return () => window.clearTimeout(timerId);
  }, []);

  useEffect(() => {
    function refreshDocumentInfo() {
      if (isGeneratingRef.current) {
        return;
      }

      const next = readDocumentInfo();

      setDocumentInfo((current) => {
        const currentKey = current?.key ?? "";
        const nextKey = next?.key ?? "";
        const currentTitle = current?.title ?? "";
        const nextTitle = next?.title ?? "";

        if (currentKey === nextKey && currentTitle === nextTitle) {
          return current;
        }

        return next;
      });
    }

    refreshDocumentInfo();
    const timerId = window.setInterval(refreshDocumentInfo, 1500);
```

    const timerId = window.setInterval(refreshDocumentInfo, 1500);

  useEffect(() => {
    const controller = new AbortController();

    void loadBootstrapSettings(controller.signal).then((bootstrapSettings) => {
      if (!bootstrapSettings) {
        return;
      }

      setSettings((current) => mergeBootstrapSettings(current, bootstrapSettings));
    });

    return () => controller.abort();
  }, []);

  useEffect(() => {
    const textarea = promptRef.current;
    if (!textarea) {
      return;
    }

    textarea.style.height = "0px";
    textarea.style.height = `${Math.min(textarea.scrollHeight, 220)}px`;
  }, [prompt]);

  useEffect(() => {
    const thread = threadRef.current;
    if (!thread || !shouldStickToBottomRef.current) {
      return;
    }

    thread.scrollTop = thread.scrollHeight;
  }, [messages]);

  useEffect(() => {
    if (!showSettings && !showModelLab && !showLogs) {
      return undefined;
    }

    function onKeyDown(event) {
      if (event.key === "Escape") {
        setShowSettings(false);
        setShowModelLab(false);
        setShowLogs(false);
      }
    }

    window.addEventListener("keydown", onKeyDown);

        }
      }

  function handlePromptKeyDown(event) {
    if (event.key === "Enter" && !event.shiftKey) {
      event.preventDefault();
      if (!isGenerating) {
        void handleGenerate();
      }
    }
  }

  function clearConversation() {
    if (isGenerating) {
      return;
    }

    if (documentInfo?.key) {
      void deleteDocumentSession(documentInfo).catch(() => {});
    }

    setMessages([]);
    setError("");
    liveSinkRef.current = null;
  }

_**Agent (gpt-5.5 2026-04-25 08:09:44Z)**_

<tool-use data-tool-type="write" data-tool-name="apply_patch"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **apply_patch**</summary>
**Add: `scripts/send-wps-ai-prompt.ps1`**

```diff
+param(
+  [string]$TitleLike = "WPS цЦЗхнЧ",
+  [string]$Prompt,
+  [int]$InputX = 1270,
+  [int]$InputY = 760,
+  [int]$SendX = 1500,
+  [int]$SendY = 770
+)
+
+$ErrorActionPreference = "Stop"
+
+if ([string]::IsNullOrWhiteSpace($Prompt)) {
+  throw "Prompt is required."
+}
+
+Add-Type -AssemblyName System.Windows.Forms
+Add-Type @"
+using System;
+using System.Runtime.InteropServices;
+
+public class WpsDesktopInput {
+  [DllImport("user32.dll")]
+  public static extern bool SetForegroundWindow(IntPtr hWnd);
+
+  [DllImport("user32.dll")]
+  public static extern bool SetCursorPos(int x, int y);
+
+  [DllImport("user32.dll")]
+  public static extern void mouse_event(uint dwFlags, uint dx, uint dy, uint dwData, UIntPtr dwExtraInfo);
+}
+"@
+
+$process = Get-Process -Name wps -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue |
+  Where-Object { $_.MainWindowHandle -ne 0 -and $_.MainWindowTitle -like "*$TitleLike*" } |
+  Sort-Object StartTime -Descending |
+  Select-Object -First 1
+
+if (-not $process) {
+  throw "No WPS window matched $TitleLike."
+}
+
+$MOUSEEVENTF_LEFTDOWN = 0x0002
+$MOUSEEVENTF_LEFTUP = 0x0004
+
+function Click-Point($x, $y) {
+  [WpsDesktopInput]::SetCursorPos($x, $y) | Out-Null
+  Start-Sleep -Milliseconds 120
+  [WpsDesktopInput]::mouse_event($MOUSEEVENTF_LEFTDOWN, 0, 0, 0, [UIntPtr]::Zero)
+  Start-Sleep -Milliseconds 80
+  [WpsDesktopInput]::mouse_event($MOUSEEVENTF_LEFTUP, 0, 0, 0, [UIntPtr]::Zero)
+}
+
+[WpsDesktopInput]::SetForegroundWindow($process.MainWindowHandle) | Out-Null
+Start-Sleep -Milliseconds 300
+Set-Clipboard -Value $Prompt
+Click-Point $InputX $InputY
+Start-Sleep -Milliseconds 250
+[System.Windows.Forms.SendKeys]::SendWait("^v")
+Start-Sleep -Milliseconds 500
+Click-Point $SendX $SendY
+Start-Sleep -Milliseconds 500
+
+Write-Output ("PROMPT_SENT_TO={0}" -f $process.MainWindowTitle)
```
</details></tool-use>

 - Run `.py`, `.js`, `.mjs`, `.bat`, or `.cmd` with their own executable, not `powershell -File`.
 - Use `rtk <program> ...` directly when PowerShell is unnecessary, for example `rtk git status`, `rtk npm test`, `rtk node script.mjs`.
+- For `.ps1` scripts that may run under Windows PowerShell 5, keep parameter defaults and control strings ASCII, or save explicitly as UTF-8 with BOM; otherwise non-ASCII defaults can be decoded incorrectly.

 1 тФВ # RTK - Rust Token Killer (Codex CLI)
 2 тФВ 
 3 тФВ **Usage**: Token-optimized CLI proxy for shell commands.
 4 тФВ 
 5 тФВ ## Rule
 6 тФВ 
 7 тФВ Always prefix shell commands with `rtk`.
 8 тФВ 
 9 тФВ Examples:
10 тФВ 
11 тФВ ```bash
12 тФВ rtk git status
13 тФВ rtk cargo test
14 тФВ rtk npm run build
15 тФВ rtk pytest -q
16 тФВ ```
17 тФВ 
18 тФВ ## Meta Commands
19 тФВ 
20 тФВ ```bash
21 тФВ rtk gain            # Token savings analytics
22 тФВ rtk gain --history  # Recent command savings history
23 тФВ rtk proxy <cmd>     # Run raw command without filtering
24 тФВ ```
25 тФВ 
26 тФВ ## Verification
27 тФВ 
28 тФВ ```bash
29 тФВ rtk --version
30 тФВ rtk gain
31 тФВ which rtk
32 тФВ ```
```

_**Agent (gpt-5.5 2026-04-25 08:15:32Z)**_

<tool-use data-tool-type="shell" data-tool-name="shell_command"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **shell_command** `rtk read -n --max-lines 260 C:\Users\10297441.WIN-9DOP5T7GHM7\.codex\skills\rtk-usage\SKILL.md`</summary>

Output:
  1 тФВ ---
  2 тФВ name: rtk-usage
  3 тФВ description: Use when working with `rtk` on Windows or Codex, especially for command selection, validating `rtk` behavior, fixing `rtk` failures, diagnosing `No hook installed`, or avoiding incorrect fallback to raw shell commands. Trigger for `rtk` installation, upgrade, init, hook, rewrite, proxy, env, json, verify, gain, and command-usage troubleshooting.
  4 тФВ ---
  5 тФВ 
  6 тФВ # RTK Usage
  7 тФВ 
  8 тФВ Use this skill whenever a task depends on `rtk` working correctly.
  9 тФВ 
 10 тФВ ## Core Rule
 11 тФВ 
 12 тФВ 1. If an `rtk` command fails, do not fall back to the equivalent raw shell command.
 13 тФВ 2. Diagnose and fix the `rtk` issue first.
 14 тФВ 3. Only continue the main task after `rtk` is working again.
 15 тФВ 4. Prefer RTK-native subcommands over `rtk run` / `rtk proxy` when a native subcommand exists.
 16 тФВ 5. On Windows, quote carefully in the outer shell; many apparent RTK failures are PowerShell quoting errors.
 17 тФВ 6. If a non-interactive command can be run through `rtk`, run it through `rtk`.
 18 тФВ 7. Raw shell is allowed only when exact byte-for-byte output matters, the command is tiny/trivial, the command is interactive, or `rtk` would change semantics.
 19 тФВ 8. When updating this skill, add only correct reusable RTK command forms and decision rules.
 20 тФВ 
 21 тФВ ## Windows Baseline Checks
 22 тФВ 
 23 тФВ 1. Run `rtk --version`.
 24 тФВ 2. Run `rtk --help`.
 25 тФВ 3. Run `rtk gain`.
 26 тФВ 4. Run `rtk init -g --show` for Claude hook status.
 27 тФВ 5. Run `rtk init -g --codex --show` for Codex global status.
 28 тФВ 6. Run `rtk verify`.
 29 тФВ 7. Run `rtk proxy where.exe rtk` to confirm the active binary.
 30 тФВ 8. On Windows release installs, prefer the official GitHub asset `rtk-x86_64-pc-windows-msvc.zip`.
 31 тФВ 
 32 тФВ Known-good Windows baseline observed for `rtk 0.37.2`:
 33 тФВ 
 34 тФВ ```bash
 35 тФВ rtk --version
 36 тФВ rtk --help
 37 тФВ rtk gain
 38 тФВ rtk verify
 39 тФВ rtk init -g --show
 40 тФВ rtk init -g --codex --show
 41 тФВ rtk proxy where.exe rtk
 42 тФВ ```
 43 тФВ 
 44 тФВ ## Correct Command Patterns
 45 тФВ 
 46 тФВ Use these forms exactly:
 47 тФВ 
 48 тФВ ```bash
 49 тФВ rtk env --filter PATH
 50 тФВ rtk json package.json
 51 тФВ rtk rewrite "git status --short"
 52 тФВ rtk proxy git status --short
 53 тФВ rtk proxy powershell -NoProfile -Command "Get-Content package.json -TotalCount 20"
 54 тФВ rtk powershell -NoProfile -Command "Get-Content package.json -TotalCount 20"
 55 тФВ rtk run "git branch --show-current"
 56 тФВ rtk run -c "git status --short"
 57 тФВ rtk verify
 58 тФВ rtk help hook
 59 тФВ rtk hook check git status --short
 60 тФВ ```
 61 тФВ 
 62 тФВ Rules:
 63 тФВ 
 64 тФВ 1. `rtk env` uses `--filter`; do not pass variable names as positional arguments.
 65 тФВ 2. `rtk json` takes a JSON file path; do not pass inline JSON text.
 66 тФВ 3. `rtk rewrite` can exit non-zero even after printing a valid rewrite in some Windows builds; judge by printed output and `rtk rewrite --help`, not by code alone.
 67 тФВ 4. `rtk proxy` is acceptable for diagnosis because it still goes through `rtk`.
 68 тФВ 5. `rtk run` uses shell execution semantics; prefer RTK-native commands first, then `rtk proxy` when quoting is simpler.
 69 тФВ 6. Do not use `rtk read --line` or `--line-count`; current `rtk read` supports `--max-lines`, `--tail-lines`, and `--line-numbers`.
 70 тФВ 7. `rtk powershell ...` is valid when PowerShell is the intended program; `rtk proxy powershell ...` is also acceptable for diagnosis.
 71 тФВ 8. On this Windows environment, use `powershell`, not `pwsh`, unless `rtk proxy where.exe pwsh` confirms PowerShell 7 is installed.
 72 тФВ 
 73 тФВ ## Correct Windows Recipes
 74 тФВ 
 75 тФВ Use these positive patterns for recurring Windows command scenarios:
 76 тФВ 
 77 тФВ 1. PowerShell on this machine: use `rtk powershell ...` by default; only use `pwsh` after `rtk proxy where.exe pwsh` confirms it exists.
 78 тФВ 2. Inline PowerShell containing `$`, `$env:...`, or `$_`: use outer single quotes around the PowerShell script, or escape each `$` as `` `$ `` when outer double quotes are unavoidable.
 79 тФВ 3. PowerShell regex alternation: quote the regex inside the PowerShell script, for example `-match "node|vite|wps"`.
 80 тФВ 4. Python scripts: run with `rtk python script.py`; do not use PowerShell as the interpreter.
 81 тФВ 5. Node scripts: run with `rtk node script.mjs` or the project package wrapper, not through PowerShell unless shell features are needed.
 82 тФВ 6. PowerShell scripts: run with `rtk powershell -NoProfile -ExecutionPolicy Bypass -File .\script.ps1`.
 83 тФВ 7. Builds with post-build installers: use the project wrapper through `rtk`, then verify generated artifact timestamps and install directories if the command takes longer than expected.
 84 тФВ 8. Command diagnosis: classify failures as RTK resolution, Pow
... (truncated)
```
</details></tool-use>

<tool-use data-tool-type="shell" data-tool-name="shell_command"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **shell_command** `rtk read -n --max-lines 240 C:\Users\10297441.WIN-9DOP5T7GHM7\.codex\skills\windows-powershell\SKILL.md`</summary>
```
Exit code: 0
Wall time: 0.8 seconds
Output:
  1 тФВ ---
  2 тФВ name: windows-powershell
  3 тФВ description: Use when composing or running Windows PowerShell commands from Codex, especially through `rtk powershell`, `powershell -Command`, `powershell -File`, commands with `$_`, pipes, quotes, paths with spaces, environment variables, or scripts. Prevents repeated quoting, escaping, and script invocation mistakes on Windows.
  4 тФВ ---
  5 тФВ 
  6 тФВ # Windows PowerShell
  7 тФВ 
  8 тФВ Use this skill before running non-trivial PowerShell commands on Windows. Keep updates concise: add only correct reusable command forms and decision rules, not failing commands or incident notes.
  9 тФВ 
 10 тФВ ## Command Review
 11 тФВ 
 12 тФВ Before executing, check:
 13 тФВ 
 14 тФВ - Entry mode: `rtk powershell -Command` for inline script, `rtk powershell -File` only for `.ps1`.
 15 тФВ - Write scope: target files, install directories, generated artifacts, processes.
 16 тФВ - Fragile tokens: `$_`, `$env:...`, `|`, `>`, `<`, `&`, `;`, quotes, paths with spaces.
 17 тФВ - Whether a small read-only probe should run before a write/copy/remove/build/install command.
 18 тФВ 
 19 тФВ ## Entry Rules
 20 тФВ 
 21 тФВ - Run PowerShell scripts as:
 22 тФВ 
 23 тФВ ```powershell
 24 тФВ rtk powershell -NoProfile -ExecutionPolicy Bypass -File ".\scripts\task.ps1"
 25 тФВ ```
 26 тФВ 
 27 тФВ - Run `.py`, `.js`, `.mjs`, `.bat`, or `.cmd` with their own executable, not `powershell -File`.
 28 тФВ - Use `rtk <program> ...` directly when PowerShell is unnecessary, for example `rtk git status`, `rtk npm test`, `rtk node script.mjs`.
 29 тФВ - For `.ps1` scripts that may run under Windows PowerShell 5, keep parameter defaults and control strings ASCII, or save explicitly as UTF-8 with BOM; otherwise non-ASCII defaults can be decoded incorrectly.
 30 тФВ 
 31 тФВ ## Quoting Rules
 32 тФВ 
 33 тФВ Prefer outer single quotes when inline PowerShell contains `$`, `$env:...`, or `$_`:
 34 тФВ 
 35 тФВ ```powershell
 36 тФВ rtk powershell -NoProfile -Command 'Get-Process | Where-Object { $_.ProcessName -match "node|vite|wps" } | Select-Object Id,ProcessName'
 37 тФВ ```
 38 тФВ 
 39 тФВ ```powershell
 40 тФВ rtk powershell -NoProfile -Command '$p = Join-Path $env:APPDATA "kingsoft\wps"; Get-ChildItem -LiteralPath $p'
 41 тФВ ```
 42 тФВ 
 43 тФВ When outer double quotes are required by the caller, escape every PowerShell `$` as backtick-dollar:
 44 тФВ 
 45 тФВ ```powershell
 46 тФВ rtk powershell -NoProfile -Command "`$target = Join-Path `$env:APPDATA 'kingsoft\wps\jsaddons'; Get-ChildItem -LiteralPath `$target -Directory | Select-Object Name,LastWriteTime"
 47 тФВ ```
 48 тФВ 
 49 тФВ For `$_` with outer double quotes, escape `$_` and quote regex alternation inside the script:
 50 тФВ 
 51 тФВ ```powershell
 52 тФВ rtk powershell -NoProfile -Command "Get-Process | Where-Object { `$_.ProcessName -match 'node|vite|wps|powershell' } | Select-Object Id,ProcessName,StartTime"
 53 тФВ ```
 54 тФВ 
 55 тФВ For nested quotes in WMI/CIM filters, assign the filter to a variable first:
 56 тФВ 
 57 тФВ ```powershell
 58 тФВ rtk powershell -NoProfile -Command "`$filter = 'Name = ''powershell.exe'''; Get-CimInstance Win32_Process -Filter `$filter | Select-Object ProcessId,CommandLine"
 59 тФВ ```
 60 тФВ 
 61 тФВ ## Paths And Files
 62 тФВ 
 63 тФВ - Use `-LiteralPath` for exact paths, especially paths with spaces, brackets, wildcards, or non-ASCII characters.
 64 тФВ - Quote all Windows paths.
 65 тФВ - Prefer `Join-Path` for paths built from `$env:APPDATA`, `$HOME`, or workspace variables.
 66 тФВ 
 67 тФВ Read a file:
 68 тФВ 
 69 тФВ ```powershell
 70 тФВ rtk powershell -NoProfile -Command 'Get-Content -LiteralPath "C:\path\file.txt"'
 71 тФВ ```
 72 тФВ 
 73 тФВ List selected files:
 74 тФВ 
 75 тФВ ```powershell
 76 тФВ rtk powershell -NoProfile -Command 'Get-ChildItem -LiteralPath "C:\path" -Force | Select-Object Name,LastWriteTime,Length'
 77 тФВ ```
 78 тФВ 
 79 тФВ Inspect generated or installed artifacts under `%APPDATA%`:
 80 тФВ 
 81 тФВ ```powershell
 82 тФВ rtk powershell -NoProfile -Command "`$asset = Join-Path `$env:APPDATA 'kingsoft\wps\jsaddons\wps-ai_1.0.4\assets'; Get-ChildItem -LiteralPath `$asset -Filter 'taskpane-*.js' | Select-Object Name,LastWriteTime,Length"
 83 тФВ ```
 84 тФВ 
 85 тФВ For timestamp checks where the target directory is already specific, list and sort the target directly instead of embedding complex regex filters:
 86 тФВ 
 87 тФВ ```powershell
 88 тФВ rtk powershell -NoProfile -Command '$target = Join-Path $env:APPDATA "kingsoft\wps\jsaddons\wps-ai_1.0.4"; Get-ChildItem -LiteralPath $target -Recurse -File | Sort-Object LastWriteTime -Descending | Select-Object -First 12 FullName,LastWriteTime,Length'
 89 тФВ ```
 90 тФВ 
 91 тФВ ## Write Commands
 92 тФВ 
 93 тФВ Before `Copy-Item`, `Remove-Item`, `Move-Item`, `Set-Content`, process kills, installers, or builds that copy output:
 94 тФВ 
 95 тФВ - Resolv
... (truncated)
```
</details></tool-use>

 4 тФВ $doc = $app.ActiveDocument
 5 тФВ 
 6 тФВ Write-Output ("DOCNAME=" + $doc.Name)
 7 тФВ 
 8 тФВ $tableCount = 0
 9 тФВ try {
10 тФВ   $tableCount = $doc.Tables.Count
11 тФВ } catch {
12 тФВ   $tableCount = 0
13 тФВ }
14 тФВ 
15 тФВ Write-Output ("TABLES=" + $tableCount)
16 тФВ 
17 тФВ if ($tableCount -gt 0) {
18 тФВ   $table = $doc.Tables.Item(1)
19 тФВ   Write-Output ("ROWS=" + $table.Rows.Count)
20 тФВ   Write-Output ("COLS=" + $table.Columns.Count)
21 тФВ 
22 тФВ   for ($r = 1; $r -le $table.Rows.Count; $r++) {
23 тФВ     for ($c = 1; $c -le $table.Columns.Count; $c++) {
24 тФВ       $text = $table.Cell($r, $c).Range.Text
25 тФВ       Write-Output ("CELL[$r,$c]=" + $text)
26 тФВ     }
27 тФВ   }
28 тФВ }
29 тФВ 
30 тФВ Write-Output ("TEXT=" + $doc.Content.Text)
```
</details></tool-use>

```
Exit code: 0
Wall time: 0.5 seconds
Output:
  1 тФВ ---
  2 тФВ name: skill-creator
  3 тФВ description: Guide for creating effective skills. This skill should be used when users want to create a new skill (or update an existing skill) that extends Codex's capabilities with specialized knowledge, workflows, or tool integrations.
  4 тФВ metadata:
  5 тФВ   short-description: Create or update a skill
  6 тФВ ---
  7 тФВ 
  8 тФВ # Skill Creator
  9 тФВ 
 10 тФВ This skill provides guidance for creating effective skills.
 11 тФВ 
 12 тФВ ## About Skills
 13 тФВ 
 14 тФВ Skills are modular, self-contained folders that extend Codex's capabilities by providing
 15 тФВ specialized knowledge, workflows, and tools. Think of them as "onboarding guides" for specific
 16 тФВ domains or tasksтАФthey transform Codex from a general-purpose agent into a specialized agent
 17 тФВ equipped with procedural knowledge that no model can fully possess.
 18 тФВ 
 19 тФВ ### What Skills Provide
 20 тФВ 
 21 тФВ 1. Specialized workflows - Multi-step procedures for specific domains
 22 тФВ 2. Tool integrations - Instructions for working with specific file formats or APIs
 23 тФВ 3. Domain expertise - Company-specific knowledge, schemas, business logic
 24 тФВ 4. Bundled resources - Scripts, references, and assets for complex and repetitive tasks
 25 тФВ 
 26 тФВ ## Core Principles
 27 тФВ 
 28 тФВ ### Concise is Key
 29 тФВ 
 30 тФВ The context window is a public good. Skills share the context window with everything else Codex needs: system prompt, conversation history, other Skills' metadata, and the actual user request.
 31 тФВ 
 32 тФВ **Default assumption: Codex is already very smart.** Only add context Codex doesn't already have. Challenge each piece of information: "Does Codex really need this explanation?" and "Does this paragraph justify its token cost?"
 33 тФВ 
 34 тФВ Prefer concise examples over verbose explanations.
 35 тФВ 
 36 тФВ ### Set Appropriate Degrees of Freedom
 37 тФВ 
 38 тФВ Match the level of specificity to the task's fragility and variability:
 39 тФВ 
 40 тФВ **High freedom (text-based instructions)**: Use when multiple approaches are valid, decisions depend on context, or heuristics guide the approach.
 41 тФВ 
 42 тФВ **Medium freedom (pseudocode or scripts with parameters)**: Use when a preferred pattern exists, some variation is acceptable, or configuration affects behavior.
 43 тФВ 
 44 тФВ **Low freedom (specific scripts, few parameters)**: Use when operations are fragile and error-prone, consistency is critical, or a specific sequence must be followed.
 45 тФВ 
 46 тФВ Think of Codex as exploring a path: a narrow bridge with cliffs needs specific guardrails (low freedom), while an open field allows many routes (high freedom).
 47 тФВ 
 48 тФВ ### Protect Validation Integrity
 49 тФВ 
 50 тФВ You may use subagents during iteration to validate whether a skill works on realistic tasks or whether a suspected problem is real. This is most useful when you want an independent pass on the skill's behavior, outputs, or failure modes after a revision.  Only do this when it is possible to start new subagents.
 51 тФВ 
 52 тФВ When using subagents for validation, treat that as an evaluation surface. The goal is to learn whether the skill generalizes, not whether another agent can reconstruct the answer from leaked context.
 53 тФВ 
 54 тФВ Prefer raw artifacts such as example prompts, outputs, diffs, logs, or traces. Give the minimum task-local context needed to perform the validation. Avoid passing the intended answer, suspected bug, intended fix, or your prior conclusions unless the validation explicitly requires them.
 55 тФВ 
 56 тФВ ### Anatomy of a Skill
 57 тФВ 
 58 тФВ Every skill consists of a required SKILL.md file and optional bundled resources:
 59 тФВ 
 60 тФВ ```
 61 тФВ skill-name/
 62 тФВ тФЬтФАтФА SKILL.md (required)
 63 тФВ тФВ   тФЬтФАтФА YAML frontmatter metadata (required)
 64 тФВ тФВ   тФВ   тФЬтФАтФА name: (required)
 65 тФВ тФВ   тФВ   тФФтФАтФА description: (required)
 66 тФВ тФВ   тФФтФАтФА Markdown instructions (required)
 67 тФВ тФЬтФАтФА agents/ (recommended)
 68 тФВ тФВ   тФФтФАтФА openai.yaml - UI metadata for skill lists and chips
 69 тФВ тФФтФАтФА Bundled Resources (optional)
 70 тФВ     тФЬтФАтФА scripts/          - Executable code (Python/Bash/etc.)
 71 тФВ     тФЬтФАтФА references/       - Documentation intended to be loaded into context as needed
 72 тФВ     тФФтФАтФА assets/           - Files used in output (templates, icons, fonts, etc.)
 73 тФВ ```
 74 тФВ 
 75 тФВ #### SKILL.md (required)
 76 тФВ 
 77 тФВ Every SKILL.md consists of:
 78 тФВ 
 79 тФВ - **Frontmatter** (YAML): Contains `name` and `description` fields. These are the only fields that Codex reads to determine when the skill gets used, thus it is very important to be clear and comprehensive in describing what the skill is, and when it should be used.
 80 тФВ - **Body** (Markdown): Instructions and guidance f
... (truncated)
```
</details></tool-use>

<tool-use data-tool-type="write" data-tool-name="apply_patch"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **apply_patch**</summary>
**Update: `scripts/send-wps-ai-prompt.ps1`**

```diff
@@
   [string]$TitleLike = "WPS",
   [string]$Prompt,
   [string]$PromptPath = "",
-  [int]$InputX = 1270,
-  [int]$InputY = 760,
-  [int]$SendX = 1500,
-  [int]$SendY = 770
+  [int]$InputX = 0,
+  [int]$InputY = 0,
+  [int]$SendX = 0,
+  [int]$SendY = 0
 )
@@
 if (-not [string]::IsNullOrWhiteSpace($PromptPath)) {
-  $Prompt = Get-Content -LiteralPath $PromptPath -Raw
+  $resolvedPromptPath = (Resolve-Path -LiteralPath $PromptPath).Path
+  $Prompt = [System.IO.File]::ReadAllText(
+    $resolvedPromptPath,
+    [System.Text.UTF8Encoding]::new($false, $true)
+  )
 }
@@
 Add-Type -AssemblyName System.Windows.Forms
+Add-Type -AssemblyName UIAutomationClient
+Add-Type -AssemblyName UIAutomationTypes
 Add-Type @"
@@
 if (-not $process) {
   throw "No WPS window matched $TitleLike."
 }
 
+$root = [System.Windows.Automation.AutomationElement]::FromHandle($process.MainWindowHandle)
+$cefPane = if ($root) {
+  $root.FindFirst(
+    [System.Windows.Automation.TreeScope]::Descendants,
+    [System.Windows.Automation.PropertyCondition]::new(
+      [System.Windows.Automation.AutomationElement]::ClassNameProperty,
+      "CefBrowserWindow"
+    )
+  )
+} else {
+  $null
+}
+
+if ($cefPane) {
+  $rect = $cefPane.Current.BoundingRectangle
+  if ($InputX -le 0) {
+    $InputX = [int]($rect.X + ($rect.Width * 0.5))
+  }
+  if ($InputY -le 0) {
+    $InputY = [int]($rect.Y + $rect.Height - 52)
+  }
+  if ($SendX -le 0) {
+    $SendX = [int]($rect.X + $rect.Width - 36)
+  }
+  if ($SendY -le 0) {
+    $SendY = [int]($rect.Y + $rect.Height - 40)
+  }
+}
+
+if ($InputX -le 0 -or $InputY -le 0 -or $SendX -le 0 -or $SendY -le 0) {
+  throw "Taskpane coordinates could not be resolved."
+}
+
 $MOUSEEVENTF_LEFTDOWN = 0x0002
 $MOUSEEVENTF_LEFTUP = 0x0004
@@
 Write-Output ("PROMPT_SENT_TO={0}" -f $process.MainWindowTitle)
+Write-Output ("INPUT={0},{1}" -f $InputX, $InputY)
+Write-Output ("SEND={0},{1}" -f $SendX, $SendY)
```
</details></tool-use>

<tool-use data-tool-type="write" data-tool-name="apply_patch"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **apply_patch**</summary>
**Add: `scripts/show-wps-test-status.ps1`**

```diff
+param(
+  [Parameter(Mandatory = $true)]
+  [string]$StatusPath,
+  [int]$RefreshMs = 400
+)
+
+$ErrorActionPreference = "Stop"
+
+Add-Type -AssemblyName System.Windows.Forms
+Add-Type -AssemblyName System.Drawing
+
+function Get-StatusColor($name) {
+  switch ($name) {
+    "green" { return [System.Drawing.Color]::FromArgb(28, 122, 72) }
+    "orange" { return [System.Drawing.Color]::FromArgb(190, 103, 12) }
+    "red" { return [System.Drawing.Color]::FromArgb(176, 32, 32) }
+    "gray" { return [System.Drawing.Color]::FromArgb(82, 91, 107) }
+    default { return [System.Drawing.Color]::FromArgb(0, 102, 204) }
+  }
+}
+
+function Read-Status {
+  if (-not (Test-Path -LiteralPath $StatusPath)) {
+    return [pscustomobject]@{
+      title = "WPS AI UI Test"
+      message = "Starting..."
+      color = "blue"
+      done = $false
+    }
+  }
+
+  try {
+    $json = [System.IO.File]::ReadAllText($StatusPath, [System.Text.Encoding]::UTF8)
+    return $json | ConvertFrom-Json
+  } catch {
+    return [pscustomobject]@{
+      title = "WPS AI UI Test"
+      message = "Reading status..."
+      color = "gray"
+      done = $false
+    }
+  }
+}
+
+$form = New-Object System.Windows.Forms.Form
+$form.Text = "WPS AI UI Test"
+$form.Width = 420
+$form.Height = 128
+$form.FormBorderStyle = [System.Windows.Forms.FormBorderStyle]::FixedToolWindow
+$form.TopMost = $true
+$form.ShowInTaskbar = $false
+$form.StartPosition = [System.Windows.Forms.FormStartPosition]::Manual
+
+$screen = [System.Windows.Forms.Screen]::PrimaryScreen.WorkingArea
+$form.Left = $screen.Right - $form.Width - 24
+$form.Top = $screen.Bottom - $form.Height - 40
+
+$titleLabel = New-Object System.Windows.Forms.Label
+$titleLabel.Left = 18
+$titleLabel.Top = 14
+$titleLabel.Width = 370
+$titleLabel.Height = 24
+$titleLabel.Font = New-Object System.Drawing.Font("Segoe UI", 12, [System.Drawing.FontStyle]::Bold)
+$titleLabel.ForeColor = [System.Drawing.Color]::White
+
+$messageLabel = New-Object System.Windows.Forms.Label
+$messageLabel.Left = 18
+$messageLabel.Top = 45
+$messageLabel.Width = 370
+$messageLabel.Height = 48
+$messageLabel.Font = New-Object System.Drawing.Font("Segoe UI", 9)
+$messageLabel.ForeColor = [System.Drawing.Color]::White
+
+$form.Controls.Add($titleLabel)
+$form.Controls.Add($messageLabel)
+
+$closeAt = $null
+$timer = New-Object System.Windows.Forms.Timer
+$timer.Interval = [Math]::Max(200, $RefreshMs)
+$timer.Add_Tick({
+  $status = Read-Status
+  $titleLabel.Text = [string]($status.title ?? "WPS AI UI Test")
+  $messageLabel.Text = [string]($status.message ?? "")
+  $form.BackColor = Get-StatusColor ([string]($status.color ?? "blue"))
+
+  if ($status.done -eq $true) {
+    if ($null -eq $script:closeAt) {
+      $script:closeAt = (Get-Date).AddSeconds(4)
+    } elseif ((Get-Date) -ge $script:closeAt) {
+      $timer.Stop()
+      $form.Close()
+    }
+  } else {
+    $script:closeAt = $null
+  }
+})
+
+$timer.Start()
+[System.Windows.Forms.Application]::Run($form)
```
</details></tool-use>
