<tool-use data-tool-type="write" data-tool-name="apply_patch"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **apply_patch**</summary>
**Add: `scripts/run-wps-ai-ui-test.ps1`**

```diff
+param(
+  [string]$PromptPath = ".\scripts\wps-ai-render-test-prompt.txt",
+  [string]$LogRoot = ".\test-artifacts\wps-ai-ui",
+  [int]$WaitSeconds = 180,
+  [switch]$NoPopup,
+  [switch]$NoSend
+)
+
+$ErrorActionPreference = "Stop"
+
+Add-Type -AssemblyName UIAutomationClient
+Add-Type -AssemblyName UIAutomationTypes
+
+$Utf8NoBom = [System.Text.UTF8Encoding]::new($false)
+$ProjectRoot = (Resolve-Path -LiteralPath (Join-Path $PSScriptRoot "..")).Path
+$PromptFullPath = if ([System.IO.Path]::IsPathRooted($PromptPath)) {
+  (Resolve-Path -LiteralPath $PromptPath).Path
+} else {
+  (Resolve-Path -LiteralPath (Join-Path $ProjectRoot $PromptPath)).Path
+}
+
+$LogRootFullPath = if ([System.IO.Path]::IsPathRooted($LogRoot)) {
+  $LogRoot
+} else {
+  Join-Path $ProjectRoot $LogRoot
+}
+
+$RunId = Get-Date -Format "yyyyMMdd-HHmmss"
+$RunDir = Join-Path $LogRootFullPath $RunId
+New-Item -ItemType Directory -Path $RunDir -Force | Out-Null
+
+$LogPath = Join-Path $RunDir "run.log"
+$StatusPath = Join-Path $RunDir "status.json"
+$SummaryPath = Join-Path $RunDir "summary.json"
+$BeforeShot = Join-Path $RunDir "before-send.png"
+$AfterShot = Join-Path $RunDir "after-output.png"
+$StuckShot = Join-Path $RunDir "stuck.png"
+
+function Add-Log {
+  param([string]$Message)
+  $line = "[{0}] {1}" -f (Get-Date -Format "yyyy-MM-dd HH:mm:ss.fff"), $Message
+  [System.IO.File]::AppendAllText($LogPath, $line + [Environment]::NewLine, $Utf8NoBom)
+  Write-Output $line
+}
+
+function Set-TestStatus {
+  param(
+    [string]$Message,
+    [string]$Color = "blue",
+    [bool]$Done = $false
+  )
+
+  $status = [pscustomobject]@{
+    title = "WPS AI UI Test"
+    message = $Message
+    color = $Color
+    done = $Done
+    time = (Get-Date).ToString("s")
+    log = $LogPath
+  }
+
+  [System.IO.File]::WriteAllText(
+    $StatusPath,
+    ($status | ConvertTo-Json -Compress),
+    $Utf8NoBom
+  )
+
+  Add-Log ("STATUS {0}: {1}" -f $Color, $Message)
+}
+
+function Start-StatusPopup {
+  if ($NoPopup) {
+    return
+  }
+
+  $scriptPath = Join-Path $PSScriptRoot "show-wps-test-status.ps1"
+  Start-Process -FilePath "powershell" -WindowStyle Hidden -ArgumentList @(
+    "-NoProfile",
+    "-ExecutionPolicy",
+    "Bypass",
+    "-File",
+    $scriptPath,
+    "-StatusPath",
+    $StatusPath
+  ) | Out-Null
+}
+
+function Invoke-Step {
+  param(
+    [string]$Name,
+    [scriptblock]$Action
+  )
+
+  Add-Log ("STEP START {0}" -f $Name)
+  try {
+    $result = & $Action
+    Add-Log ("STEP OK {0}" -f $Name)
+    return $result
+  } catch {
+    Add-Log ("STEP FAIL {0}: {1}" -f $Name, $_.Exception.Message)
+    Set-TestStatus ("Failed: {0}" -f $Name) "red" $true
+    throw
+  }
+}
+
+function Get-WpsProcessForDocument {
+  param([string]$DocumentName)
+
+  $deadline = (Get-Date).AddSeconds(20)
+  while ((Get-Date) -lt $deadline) {
+    $process = Get-Process -Name wps -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue |
+      Where-Object {
+        $_.MainWindowHandle -ne 0 -and
+        ($_.MainWindowTitle -like "*$DocumentName*" -or $_.MainWindowTitle -like "*WPS*")
+      } |
+      Sort-Object StartTime -Descending |
+      Select-Object -First 1
+
+    if ($process) {
+      return $process
+    }
+
+    Start-Sleep -Milliseconds 500
+  }
+
+  return $null
+}
+
+function Get-CefPaneRect {
+  param([System.Diagnostics.Process]$Process)
+
+  if (-not $Process -or $Process.MainWindowHandle -eq 0) {
+    return $null
+  }
+
+  $root = [System.Windows.Automation.AutomationElement]::FromHandle($Process.MainWindowHandle)
+  if (-not $root) {
+    return $null
+  }
+
+  $pane = $root.FindFirst(
+    [System.Windows.Automation.TreeScope]::Descendants,
+    [System.Windows.Automation.PropertyCondition]::new(
+      [System.Windows.Automation.AutomationElement]::ClassNameProperty,
+      "CefBrowserWindow"
+    )
+  )
+
+  if (-not $pane) {
+    return $null
+  }
+
+  return $pane.Current.BoundingRectangle
+}
+
+function Wait-Taskpane {
+  param([System.Diagnostics.Process]$Process)
+
+  $deadline = (Get-Date).AddSeconds(40)
+  while ((Get-Date) -lt $deadline) {
+    $rect = Get-CefPaneRect $Process
+    if ($rect -and -not $rect.IsEmpty -and $rect.Width -gt 200 -and $rect.Height -gt 200) {
+      return $rect
+    }
+
+    Start-Sleep -Milliseconds 800
+  }
+
+  return $null
+}
+
+function Get-DocumentText {
+  param($Document)
+  try {
+    return [string]$Document.Content.Text
+  } catch {
+    return ""
+  }
+}
+
+function Get-TableSummary {
+  param($Document)
+
+  $items = @()
+  $count = 0
+
+  try {
+    $count = [int]$Document.Tables.Count
+  } catch {
+    $count = 0
+  }
+
+  for ($i = 1; $i -le $count; $i++) {
+    try {
+      $table = $Document.Tables.Item($i)
+      $items += [pscustomobject]@{
+        index = $i
+        rows = [int]$table.Rows.Count
+        cols = [int]$table.Columns.Count
+      }
+    } catch {
+      $items += [pscustomobject]@{
+        index = $i
+        rows = 0
+        cols = 0
+      }
+    }
+  }
+
+  return [pscustomobject]@{
+    count = $count
+    items = $items
+  }
+}
+
+function Get-FormatStats {
+  param($Document)
+
+  $stats = [ordered]@{
+    sampledChars = 0
+    codeFontChars = 0
+    coloredChars = 0
+    boldChars = 0
+    italicChars = 0
+    headingLikeParagraphs = 0
+    leftAlignedParagraphs = 0
+    paragraphSamples = @()
+  }
+
+  try {
+    $paragraphCount = [Math]::Min([int]$Document.Paragraphs.Count, 16)
+    for ($i = 1; $i -le $paragraphCount; $i++) {
+      $paragraph = $Document.Paragraphs.Item($i)
+      $range = $paragraph.Range
+      $text = ([string]$range.Text -replace "\s+", " ").Trim()
+      if ($text.Length -gt 80) {
+        $text = $text.Substring(0, 80)
+      }
+
+      $fontSize = 0
+      $bold = 0
+      $alignment = ""
+      $lineSpacing = 0
+      try { $fontSize = [double]$range.Font.Size } catch {}
+      try { $bold = [int]$range.Font.Bold } catch {}
+      try { $alignment = [string]$range.ParagraphFormat.Alignment } catch {}
+      try { $lineSpacing = [double]$range.ParagraphFormat.LineSpacing } catch {}
+
+      if ($fontSize -ge 14 -or $bold -ne 0) {
+        $stats.headingLikeParagraphs++
+      }
+      if ($alignment -eq "0" -or $alignment -eq "wdAlignParagraphLeft") {
+        $stats.leftAlignedParagraphs++
+      }
+
+      $stats.paragraphSamples += [pscustomobject]@{
+        index = $i
+        text = $text
+        fontSize = $fontSize
+        bold = $bold
+        alignment = $alignment
+        lineSpacing = $lineSpacing
+      }
+    }
+  } catch {}
+
+  try {
+    $chars = $Document.Content.Characters
+    $limit = [Math]::Min([int]$chars.Count, 6000)
+    for ($i = 1; $i -le $limit; $i++) {
+      $charRange = $chars.Item($i)
+      $stats.sampledChars++
+
+      $fontName = ""
+      $fontNameAscii = ""
+      $color = $null
+      $bold = 0
+      $italic = 0
+
+      try { $fontName = [string]$charRange.Font.Name } catch {}
+      try { $fontNameAscii = [string]$charRange.Font.NameAscii } catch {}
+      try { $color = [int]$charRange.Font.Color } catch {}
+      try { $bold = [int]$charRange.Font.Bold } catch {}
+      try { $italic = [int]$charRange.Font.Italic } catch {}
+
+      if ($fontName -like "*Consolas*" -or $fontNameAscii -like "*Consolas*") {
+        $stats.codeFontChars++
+      }
+      if ($null -ne $color -and $color -ne 0 -and $color -ne -16777216) {
+        $stats.coloredChars++
+      }
+      if ($bold -ne 0) {
+        $stats.boldChars++
+      }
+      if ($italic -ne 0) {
+        $stats.italicChars++
+      }
+    }
+  } catch {}
+
+  return [pscustomobject]$stats
+}
+
+function Save-Screenshot {
+  param(
+    [System.Diagnostics.Process]$Process,
+    [string]$Path
+  )
+
+  $captureScript = Join-Path $PSScriptRoot "capture-wps-window.ps1"
+  & $captureScript -ProcessId $Process.Id -OutputPath $Path | Out-Null
+  Add-Log ("SCREENSHOT {0}" -f $Path)
+}
+
+Start-StatusPopup
+Set-TestStatus "Preparing log and checking relay..." "blue"
+
+Add-Log ("RUN_DIR={0}" -f $RunDir)
+Add-Log ("PROMPT_PATH={0}" -f $PromptFullPath)
+Add-Log ("POWERSHELL={0}" -f $PSVersionTable.PSVersion.ToString())
+
+$prompt = [System.IO.File]::ReadAllText(
+  $PromptFullPath,
+  [System.Text.UTF8Encoding]::new($false, $true)
+)
+Add-Log ("PROMPT_CHARS={0}" -f $prompt.Length)
+
+Invoke-Step "relay bootstrap" {
+  try {
+    $bootstrap = Invoke-RestMethod -Uri "http://127.0.0.1:3888/bootstrap" -TimeoutSec 8
+    Add-Log ("BOOTSTRAP_PROVIDER={0}" -f $bootstrap.defaults.providerId)
+    Add-Log ("BOOTSTRAP_MODEL={0}" -f $bootstrap.defaults.model)
+  } catch {
+    Add-Log "Relay bootstrap failed; starting relay script."
+    $relayScript = Join-Path $ProjectRoot "server\start-relay.ps1"
+    & $relayScript -ForceRestart | Out-Null
+    Start-Sleep -Seconds 3
+    $bootstrap = Invoke-RestMethod -Uri "http://127.0.0.1:3888/bootstrap" -TimeoutSec 8
+    Add-Log ("BOOTSTRAP_PROVIDER={0}" -f $bootstrap.defaults.providerId)
+    Add-Log ("BOOTSTRAP_MODEL={0}" -f $bootstrap.defaults.model)
+  }
+}
+
+Set-TestStatus "Opening a new WPS Writer document..." "blue"
+$wpsContext = Invoke-Step "open WPS document" {
+  $app = New-Object -ComObject kwps.Application
+  $app.Visible = $true
+  $doc = $app.Documents.Add()
+  Start-Sleep -Seconds 5
+  [pscustomobject]@{
+    app = $app
+    doc = $doc
+    name = [string]$doc.Name
+  }
+}
+
+Add-Log ("DOC_NAME={0}" -f $wpsContext.name)
+
+$wpsProcess = Invoke-Step "find WPS window" {
+  $process = Get-WpsProcessForDocument $wpsContext.name
+  if (-not $process) {
+    throw "No WPS process with a visible main window."
+  }
+  Add-Log ("WPS_PROCESS={0} TITLE={1}" -f $process.Id, $process.MainWindowTitle)
+  $process
+}
+
+Set-TestStatus "Waiting for the WPS AI taskpane..." "blue"
+$paneRect = Invoke-Step "wait taskpane" {
+  $rect = Wait-Taskpane $wpsProcess
+  if (-not $rect) {
+    throw "WPS AI taskpane did not become visible."
+  }
+  Add-Log ("TASKPANE_RECT={0},{1},{2},{3}" -f [int]$rect.X, [int]$rect.Y, [int]$rect.Width, [int]$rect.Height)
+  $rect
+}
+
+Save-Screenshot $wpsProcess $BeforeShot
+
+if ($NoSend) {
+  Set-TestStatus "NoSend enabled; skipping prompt submission." "orange" $true
+  exit 0
+}
+
+Set-TestStatus "Sending the fixed AI prompt to the taskpane..." "blue"
+Invoke-Step "send prompt" {
+  $sendScript = Join-Path $PSScriptRoot "send-wps-ai-prompt.ps1"
+  $output = & $sendScript -TitleLike $wpsContext.name -PromptPath $PromptFullPath
+  foreach ($line in $output) {
+    Add-Log ("SEND_OUTPUT {0}" -f $line)
+  }
+}
+
+Set-TestStatus "Waiting for AI output in the WPS document..." "blue"
+$monitorStart = Get-Date
+$deadline = $monitorStart.AddSeconds($WaitSeconds)
+$lastLength = -1
+$stableRounds = 0
+$contentStarted = $false
+
+while ((Get-Date) -lt $deadline) {
+  $text = Get-DocumentText $wpsContext.doc
+  $length = $text.Length
+  $tables = Get-TableSummary $wpsContext.doc
+
+  if ($length -ne $lastLength) {
+    Add-Log ("DOC_PROGRESS chars={0} tables={1}" -f $length, $tables.count)
+    $lastLength = $length
+    $stableRounds = 0
+  } elseif ($length -gt 30) {
+    $stableRounds++
+  }
+
+  if ($length -gt 30) {
+    $contentStarted = $true
+  }
+
+  Set-TestStatus ("Waiting... chars={0}, tables={1}" -f $length, $tables.count) "blue"
+
+  if ($contentStarted -and $stableRounds -ge 6) {
+    break
+  }
+
+  Start-Sleep -Seconds 2
+}
+
+$finalText = Get-DocumentText $wpsContext.doc
+$finalTables = Get-TableSummary $wpsContext.doc
+
+if ($finalText.Length -le 30) {
+  Save-Screenshot $wpsProcess $StuckShot
+  Add-Log "NO_DOCUMENT_OUTPUT_AFTER_WAIT"
+  Set-TestStatus "No document output detected; see log and screenshot." "red" $true
+  throw "No WPS document output detected after waiting."
+}
+
+Set-TestStatus "Inspecting table, code highlight, headings, and body text..." "blue"
+$formatStats = Get-FormatStats $wpsContext.doc
+Save-Screenshot $wpsProcess $AfterShot
+
+$tableOk = $false
+if ($finalTables.count -ge 1) {
+  $firstTable = $finalTables.items | Select-Object -First 1
+  $tableOk = ($firstTable.rows -ge 4 -and $firstTable.cols -ge 3)
+}
+
+$summary = [pscustomobject]@{
+  runId = $RunId
+  runDir = $RunDir
+  log = $LogPath
+  document = $wpsContext.name
+  chars = $finalText.Length
+  tableCount = $finalTables.count
+  tables = $finalTables.items
+  checks = [pscustomobject]@{
+    bodyText = ($finalText.Length -gt 200)
+    headings = ($formatStats.headingLikeParagraphs -ge 2)
+    table = $tableOk
+    codeFont = ($formatStats.codeFontChars -gt 0)
+    codeColor = ($formatStats.coloredChars -gt 0)
+    bold = ($formatStats.boldChars -gt 0)
+    italic = ($formatStats.italicChars -gt 0)
+  }
+  formatStats = $formatStats
+  screenshots = [pscustomobject]@{
+    before = $BeforeShot
+    after = $AfterShot
+    stuck = $StuckShot
+  }
+}
+
+[System.IO.File]::WriteAllText(
+  $SummaryPath,
+  ($summary | ConvertTo-Json -Depth 8),
+  $Utf8NoBom
+)
+
+Add-Log ("SUMMARY={0}" -f $SummaryPath)
+Add-Log ("FINAL_CHARS={0}" -f $summary.chars)
+Add-Log ("FINAL_TABLES={0}" -f $summary.tableCount)
+Add-Log ("CODE_FONT_CHARS={0}" -f $formatStats.codeFontChars)
+Add-Log ("COLORED_CHARS={0}" -f $formatStats.coloredChars)
+Add-Log ("HEADING_LIKE_PARAGRAPHS={0}" -f $formatStats.headingLikeParagraphs)
+
+$failedChecks = @()
+foreach ($property in $summary.checks.PSObject.Properties) {
+  if ($property.Value -ne $true) {
+    $failedChecks += $property.Name
+  }
+}
+
+if ($failedChecks.Count -gt 0) {
+  Add-Log ("CHECK_WARNINGS={0}" -f ($failedChecks -join ","))
+  Set-TestStatus ("Finished with warnings: {0}" -f ($failedChecks -join ",")) "orange" $true
+} else {
+  Set-TestStatus "Finished: table, code, headings, and body checks passed." "green" $true
+}
+
+Write-Output ("RUN_DIR={0}" -f $RunDir)
+Write-Output ("LOG={0}" -f $LogPath)
+Write-Output ("SUMMARY={0}" -f $SummaryPath)
```
</details></tool-use>

@@
-$ErrorActionPreference = "Stop"
-
-$app = New-Object -ComObject kwps.Application
-$doc = $app.ActiveDocument
+param(
+  [string]$DocumentName = ""
+)
+
+$ErrorActionPreference = "Stop"
+
+try {
+  $app = [System.Runtime.InteropServices.Marshal]::GetActiveObject("kwps.Application")
+} catch {
+  $app = New-Object -ComObject kwps.Application
+}
+
+$doc = $null
+
+if (-not [string]::IsNullOrWhiteSpace($DocumentName)) {
+  for ($i = 1; $i -le $app.Documents.Count; $i++) {
+    $candidate = $app.Documents.Item($i)
+    if ([string]$candidate.Name -like "*$DocumentName*") {
+      $doc = $candidate
+      break
+    }
+  }
+}
+
+if (-not $doc) {
+  $doc = $app.ActiveDocument
+}
+
+if (-not $doc) {
+  throw "No active WPS Writer document."
+}

<tool-use data-tool-type="write" data-tool-name="apply_patch"><details>

**Add: `scripts/README-wps-ai-ui-test.md`**

```diff
+# WPS AI UI Test
+
+Use one entry point for WPS desktop testing:
+
+```powershell
+rtk powershell -NoProfile -ExecutionPolicy Bypass -File ".\scripts\run-wps-ai-ui-test.ps1"
+```
+
+The runner creates a timestamped folder under `test-artifacts/wps-ai-ui/` with:
+
+- `run.log`: full step log with timestamps.
+- `status.json`: current status consumed by the popup.
+- `summary.json`: table, code highlighting, heading, and body checks.
+- `before-send.png`, `after-output.png`, and `stuck.png` when applicable.
+
+Rules for this test flow:
+
+- Do not send ad hoc click commands first. Run the test runner so progress and failures are logged.
+- Keep `.ps1` defaults ASCII. Read prompt files with .NET UTF-8 APIs.
+- Use the taskpane `CefBrowserWindow` rectangle to derive click coordinates.
+- If output does not appear before timeout, stop and inspect the log/screenshot instead of repeating the same send command.
+- Leave the WPS document open after the run for manual inspection.
```
</details></tool-use>

<tool-use data-tool-type="write" data-tool-name="apply_patch"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **apply_patch**</summary>
**Add: `C:/Users/10297441.WIN-9DOP5T7GHM7/.codex/skills/wps-ai-testing/SKILL.md`**

```diff
+---
+name: wps-ai-testing
+description: Use when running or troubleshooting WPS-AI-CHAT desktop UI tests in WPS Writer, especially tests that must send a prompt through the WPS AI taskpane and verify WPS document rendering for headings, body text, tables, and code highlighting.
+---
+
+# WPS AI Testing
+
+Use the project test runner instead of ad hoc click commands.
+
+## Entry Point
+
+From `d:\cursor_dev\wps_ai`:
+
+```powershell
+rtk powershell -NoProfile -ExecutionPolicy Bypass -File ".\scripts\run-wps-ai-ui-test.ps1"
+```
+
+Options:
+
+```powershell
+rtk powershell -NoProfile -ExecutionPolicy Bypass -File ".\scripts\run-wps-ai-ui-test.ps1" -WaitSeconds 240
+rtk powershell -NoProfile -ExecutionPolicy Bypass -File ".\scripts\run-wps-ai-ui-test.ps1" -NoPopup
+```
+
+## Rules
+
+1. Do not start with loose `click-screen` commands. Run the runner first so every step is logged.
+2. The runner shows a colored topmost status popup and writes logs under `test-artifacts\wps-ai-ui\<timestamp>\`.
+3. The fixed prompt is `scripts\wps-ai-render-test-prompt.txt`; read prompt files with .NET UTF-8 APIs, not plain `Get-Content`, when clipboard text may contain Chinese.
+4. Use the WPS taskpane `CefBrowserWindow` rectangle to derive input/send coordinates.
+5. If output does not appear before timeout, stop and inspect `run.log`, `summary.json`, and `stuck.png`; do not repeat the same send command.
+6. Leave the generated WPS document open for manual inspection.
+
+## Validation Targets
+
+The test must check:
+
+- AI taskpane prompt submission.
+- WPS body text output length.
+- At least one real WPS table with expected dimensions.
+- Code font and colored code characters.
+- Heading-like paragraphs, bold, and italic formatting.
+- Screenshots before send and after output.
```
</details></tool-use>

@@
 - For `.ps1` scripts that may run under Windows PowerShell 5, keep parameter defaults and control strings ASCII, or save explicitly as UTF-8 with BOM; otherwise non-ASCII defaults can be decoded incorrectly.
+- For UTF-8 prompt or fixture files that may contain Chinese, read with `.NET` encoding APIs: `[System.IO.File]::ReadAllText($path, [System.Text.UTF8Encoding]::new($false, $true))`.
@@
 For timestamp checks where the target directory is already specific, list and sort the target directly instead of embedding complex regex filters:
@@
 rtk powershell -NoProfile -Command '$target = Join-Path $env:APPDATA "kingsoft\wps\jsaddons\wps-ai_1.0.4"; Get-ChildItem -LiteralPath $target -Recurse -File | Sort-Object LastWriteTime -Descending | Select-Object -First 12 FullName,LastWriteTime,Length'
 ```
+
+For WPS-AI-CHAT desktop UI tests, use the project runner instead of loose click commands:
+
+```powershell
+rtk powershell -NoProfile -ExecutionPolicy Bypass -File ".\scripts\run-wps-ai-ui-test.ps1"
+```
```
</details></tool-use>

 2 тФВ 
 3 тФВ Use one entry point for WPS desktop testing:
 4 тФВ 
 5 тФВ ```powershell
 6 тФВ rtk powershell -NoProfile -ExecutionPolicy Bypass -File ".\scripts\run-wps-ai-ui-test.ps1"
 7 тФВ ```
 8 тФВ 
 9 тФВ The runner creates a timestamped folder under `test-artifacts/wps-ai-ui/` with:
10 тФВ 
11 тФВ - `run.log`: full step log with timestamps.
12 тФВ - `status.json`: current status consumed by the popup.
13 тФВ - `summary.json`: table, code highlighting, heading, and body checks.
14 тФВ - `before-send.png`, `after-output.png`, and `stuck.png` when applicable.
15 тФВ 
16 тФВ Rules for this test flow:
17 тФВ 
18 тФВ - Do not send ad hoc click commands first. Run the test runner so progress and failures are logged.
19 тФВ - Keep `.ps1` defaults ASCII. Read prompt files with .NET UTF-8 APIs.
20 тФВ - Use the taskpane `CefBrowserWindow` rectangle to derive click coordinates.
21 тФВ - If output does not appear before timeout, stop and inspect the log/screenshot instead of repeating the same send command.
22 тФВ - Leave the WPS document open after the run for manual inspection.
```
</details></tool-use>

<tool-use data-tool-type="shell" data-tool-name="shell_command"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **shell_command** `rtk read -n --max-lines 160 C:\Users\10297441.WIN-9DOP5T7GHM7\.codex\skills\wps-ai-testing\SKILL.md`</summary>
```
Exit code: 0
Wall time: 0.8 seconds
Output:
 1 тФВ ---
 2 тФВ name: wps-ai-testing
 3 тФВ description: Use when running or troubleshooting WPS-AI-CHAT desktop UI tests in WPS Writer, especially tests that must send a prompt through the WPS AI taskpane and verify WPS document rendering for headings, body text, tables, and code highlighting.
 4 тФВ ---
 5 тФВ 
 6 тФВ # WPS AI Testing
 7 тФВ 
 8 тФВ Use the project test runner instead of ad hoc click commands.
 9 тФВ 
10 тФВ ## Entry Point
11 тФВ 
12 тФВ From `d:\cursor_dev\wps_ai`:
13 тФВ 
14 тФВ ```powershell
15 тФВ rtk powershell -NoProfile -ExecutionPolicy Bypass -File ".\scripts\run-wps-ai-ui-test.ps1"
16 тФВ ```
17 тФВ 
18 тФВ Options:
19 тФВ 
20 тФВ ```powershell
21 тФВ rtk powershell -NoProfile -ExecutionPolicy Bypass -File ".\scripts\run-wps-ai-ui-test.ps1" -WaitSeconds 240
22 тФВ rtk powershell -NoProfile -ExecutionPolicy Bypass -File ".\scripts\run-wps-ai-ui-test.ps1" -NoPopup
23 тФВ ```
24 тФВ 
25 тФВ ## Rules
26 тФВ 
27 тФВ 1. Do not start with loose `click-screen` commands. Run the runner first so every step is logged.
28 тФВ 2. The runner shows a colored topmost status popup and writes logs under `test-artifacts\wps-ai-ui\<timestamp>\`.
29 тФВ 3. The fixed prompt is `scripts\wps-ai-render-test-prompt.txt`; read prompt files with .NET UTF-8 APIs, not plain `Get-Content`, when clipboard text may contain Chinese.
30 тФВ 4. Use the WPS taskpane `CefBrowserWindow` rectangle to derive input/send coordinates.
31 тФВ 5. If output does not appear before timeout, stop and inspect `run.log`, `summary.json`, and `stuck.png`; do not repeat the same send command.
32 тФВ 6. Leave the generated WPS document open for manual inspection.
33 тФВ 
34 тФВ ## Validation Targets
35 тФВ 
36 тФВ The test must check:
37 тФВ 
38 тФВ - AI taskpane prompt submission.
39 тФВ - WPS body text output length.
40 тФВ - At least one real WPS table with expected dimensions.
41 тФВ - Code font and colored code characters.
42 тФВ - Heading-like paragraphs, bold, and italic formatting.
43 тФВ - Screenshots before send and after output.
```
</details></tool-use>

```diff
@@
 - For `.ps1` scripts that may run under Windows PowerShell 5, keep parameter defaults and control strings ASCII, or save explicitly as UTF-8 with BOM; otherwise non-ASCII defaults can be decoded incorrectly.
 - For UTF-8 prompt or fixture files that may contain Chinese, read with `.NET` encoding APIs: `[System.IO.File]::ReadAllText($path, [System.Text.UTF8Encoding]::new($false, $true))`.
+- In PowerShell functions that return objects, do not use `Write-Output` for logging; it pollutes the success pipeline. Log to a file and use `Write-Host` only for human-visible progress.
```
</details></tool-use>

 1 тФВ # RTK - Rust Token Killer (Codex CLI)
 2 тФВ 
 3 тФВ **Usage**: Token-optimized CLI proxy for shell commands.

 5 тФВ ## Rule
 6 тФВ 
 7 тФВ Always prefix shell commands with `rtk`.
 8 тФВ 
 9 тФВ Examples:
10 тФВ

17 тФВ

21 тФВ rtk gain            # Token savings analytics

Output:
  1 тФВ ---
  2 тФВ name: windows-powershell
  3 тФВ description: Use when composing or running Windows PowerShell commands from Codex, especially through `rtk powershell`, `powershell -Command`, `powershell -File`, commands with `$_`, pipes, quotes, paths with spaces, environment variables, or scripts. Prevents repeated quoting, escaping, and script invocation mistakes on Windows.
  4 тФВ ---
  5 тФВ 
  6 тФВ # Windows PowerShell
  7 тФВ 
  8 тФВ Use this skill before running non-trivial PowerShell commands on Windows. Keep updates concise: add only correct reusable command forms and decision rules, not failing commands or incident notes.
  9 тФВ 
 10 тФВ ## Command Review
 11 тФВ 
 12 тФВ Before executing, check:
 13 тФВ 
 14 тФВ - Entry mode: `rtk powershell -Command` for inline script, `rtk powershell -File` only for `.ps1`.
 15 тФВ - Write scope: target files, install directories, generated artifacts, processes.
 16 тФВ - Fragile tokens: `$_`, `$env:...`, `|`, `>`, `<`, `&`, `;`, quotes, paths with spaces.
 17 тФВ - Whether a small read-only probe should run before a write/copy/remove/build/install command.
 18 тФВ 
 19 тФВ ## Entry Rules
 20 тФВ 
 21 тФВ - Run PowerShell scripts as:
 22 тФВ 
 23 тФВ ```powershell
 24 тФВ rtk powershell -NoProfile -ExecutionPolicy Bypass -File ".\scripts\task.ps1"
 25 тФВ ```
 26 тФВ 
 27 тФВ - Run `.py`, `.js`, `.mjs`, `.bat`, or `.cmd` with their own executable, not `powershell -File`.
 28 тФВ - Use `rtk <program> ...` directly when PowerShell is unnecessary, for example `rtk git status`, `rtk npm test`, `rtk node script.mjs`.
 29 тФВ - For `.ps1` scripts that may run under Windows PowerShell 5, keep parameter defaults and control strings ASCII, or save explicitly as UTF-8 with BOM; otherwise non-ASCII defaults can be decoded incorrectly.
 30 тФВ - For UTF-8 prompt or fixture files that may contain Chinese, read with `.NET` encoding APIs: `[System.IO.File]::ReadAllText($path, [System.Text.UTF8Encoding]::new($false, $true))`.
 31 тФВ - In PowerShell functions that return objects, do not use `Write-Output` for logging; it pollutes the success pipeline. Log to a file and use `Write-Host` only for human-visible progress.
 32 тФВ 
 33 тФВ ## Quoting Rules
 34 тФВ 
 35 тФВ Prefer outer single quotes when inline PowerShell contains `$`, `$env:...`, or `$_`:
 36 тФВ 
 37 тФВ ```powershell
 38 тФВ rtk powershell -NoProfile -Command 'Get-Process | Where-Object { $_.ProcessName -match "node|vite|wps" } | Select-Object Id,ProcessName'
 39 тФВ ```
 40 тФВ 
 41 тФВ ```powershell
 42 тФВ rtk powershell -NoProfile -Command '$p = Join-Path $env:APPDATA "kingsoft\wps"; Get-ChildItem -LiteralPath $p'
 43 тФВ ```
 44 тФВ 
 45 тФВ When outer double quotes are required by the caller, escape every PowerShell `$` as backtick-dollar:
 46 тФВ 
 47 тФВ ```powershell
 48 тФВ rtk powershell -NoProfile -Command "`$target = Join-Path `$env:APPDATA 'kingsoft\wps\jsaddons'; Get-ChildItem -LiteralPath `$target -Directory | Select-Object Name,LastWriteTime"
 49 тФВ ```
 50 тФВ 
 51 тФВ For `$_` with outer double quotes, escape `$_` and quote regex alternation inside the script:
 52 тФВ 
 53 тФВ ```powershell
 54 тФВ rtk powershell -NoProfile -Command "Get-Process | Where-Object { `$_.ProcessName -match 'node|vite|wps|powershell' } | Select-Object Id,ProcessName,StartTime"
 55 тФВ ```
 56 тФВ 
 57 тФВ For nested quotes in WMI/CIM filters, assign the filter to a variable first:
 58 тФВ 
 59 тФВ ```powershell
 60 тФВ rtk powershell -NoProfile -Command "`$filter = 'Name = ''powershell.exe'''; Get-CimInstance Win32_Process -Filter `$filter | Select-Object ProcessId,CommandLine"
 61 тФВ ```
 62 тФВ 
 63 тФВ ## Paths And Files
 64 тФВ 
 65 тФВ - Use `-LiteralPath` for exact paths, especially paths with spaces, brackets, wildcards, or non-ASCII characters.
 66 тФВ - Quote all Windows paths.
 67 тФВ - Prefer `Join-Path` for paths built from `$env:APPDATA`, `$HOME`, or workspace variables.
 68 тФВ 
 69 тФВ Read a file:
 70 тФВ 
 71 тФВ ```powershell
 72 тФВ rtk powershell -NoProfile -Command 'Get-Content -LiteralPath "C:\path\file.txt"'
 73 тФВ ```
 74 тФВ 
 75 тФВ List selected files:
 76 тФВ 
 77 тФВ ```powershell
 78 тФВ rtk powershell -NoProfile -Command 'Get-ChildItem -LiteralPath "C:\path" -Force | Select-Object Name,LastWriteTime,Length'
 79 тФВ ```
 80 тФВ 
 81 тФВ Inspect generated or installed artifacts under `%APPDATA%`:
 82 тФВ 
 83 тФВ ```powershell
 84 тФВ rtk powershell -NoProfile -Command "`$asset = Join-Path `$env:APPDATA 'kingsoft\wps\jsaddons\wps-ai_1.0.4\assets'; Get-ChildItem -LiteralPath `$asset -Filter 'taskpane-*.js' | Select-Object Name,LastWriteTime,Length"
 85 тФВ ```
 86 тФВ 
 87 тФВ For timestamp checks where the target directory is already specific, list and sort the target directly instead of embedding complex regex filters:
 88 тФВ 
 89 тФВ ```powershell
 90 тФВ rtk powershell -NoProfile -Command '$target = Join-Path $env:APPDATA "kings

<tool-use data-tool-type="shell" data-tool-name="shell_command"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **shell_command** `rtk powershell -NoProfile -Command '$lines = Get-Content -LiteralPath "scripts/run-wps-ai-ui-test.ps1"; $lines[100..180]'`</summary>

    Set-TestStatus ("Failed: {0}" -f $Name) "red" $true

}

function Get-WpsProcessForDocument {
  param([string]$DocumentName)

  $deadline = (Get-Date).AddSeconds(20)
  while ((Get-Date) -lt $deadline) {
    $process = Get-Process -Name wps -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue |
      Where-Object {
        $_.MainWindowHandle -ne 0 -and
        ($_.MainWindowTitle -like "*$DocumentName*" -or $_.MainWindowTitle -like "*WPS*")
      } |
      Sort-Object StartTime -Descending |
      Select-Object -First 1

    if ($process) {
      return $process
    }

    Start-Sleep -Milliseconds 500
  }

  return $null
}

function Get-CefPaneRect {
  param([System.Diagnostics.Process]$Process)

  if (-not $Process -or $Process.MainWindowHandle -eq 0) {
    return $null
  }

  $root = [System.Windows.Automation.AutomationElement]::FromHandle($Process.MainWindowHandle)
  if (-not $root) {
    return $null
  }

  $pane = $root.FindFirst(
    [System.Windows.Automation.TreeScope]::Descendants,
    [System.Windows.Automation.PropertyCondition]::new(
      [System.Windows.Automation.AutomationElement]::ClassNameProperty,
      "CefBrowserWindow"
    )
  )

  if (-not $pane) {
    return $null
  }

  return $pane.Current.BoundingRectangle
}

function Wait-Taskpane {
  param([System.Diagnostics.Process]$Process)

  $deadline = (Get-Date).AddSeconds(40)
  while ((Get-Date) -lt $deadline) {
    $rect = Get-CefPaneRect $Process
    if ($rect -and -not $rect.IsEmpty -and $rect.Width -gt 200 -and $rect.Height -gt 200) {
      return $rect
    }

    Start-Sleep -Milliseconds 800
  }

  return $null
}

function Get-DocumentText {
  param($Document)
  try {
    return [string]$Document.Content.Text
  } catch {
    return ""
  }
}

function Get-TableSummary {
```

    Start-Sleep -Seconds 3
    $bootstrap = Invoke-RestMethod -Uri "http://127.0.0.1:3888/bootstrap" -TimeoutSec 8
    Add-Log ("BOOTSTRAP_PROVIDER={0}" -f $bootstrap.defaults.providerId)
    Add-Log ("BOOTSTRAP_MODEL={0}" -f $bootstrap.defaults.model)
  }
}

Set-TestStatus "Opening a new WPS Writer document..." "blue"
$wpsContext = Invoke-Step "open WPS document" {
  $app = New-Object -ComObject kwps.Application
  $app.Visible = $true
  $doc = $app.Documents.Add()
  Start-Sleep -Seconds 5
  [pscustomobject]@{
    app = $app
    doc = $doc
    name = [string]$doc.Name
  }
}

Add-Log ("DOC_NAME={0}" -f $wpsContext.name)

$wpsProcess = Invoke-Step "find WPS window" {
  $process = Get-WpsProcessForDocument $wpsContext.name
  if (-not $process) {
    throw "No WPS process with a visible main window."
  }
  Add-Log ("WPS_PROCESS={0} TITLE={1}" -f $process.Id, $process.MainWindowTitle)
  $process
}

Set-TestStatus "Waiting for the WPS AI taskpane..." "blue"
$paneRect = Invoke-Step "wait taskpane" {
  $rect = Wait-Taskpane $wpsProcess
  if (-not $rect) {
    throw "WPS AI taskpane did not become visible."
  }
  Add-Log ("TASKPANE_RECT={0},{1},{2},{3}" -f [int]$rect.X, [int]$rect.Y, [int]$rect.Width, [int]$rect.Height)
  $rect
}

Save-Screenshot $wpsProcess $BeforeShot

if ($NoSend) {
  Set-TestStatus "NoSend enabled; skipping prompt submission." "orange" $true
  exit 0
}

Set-TestStatus "Sending the fixed AI prompt to the taskpane..." "blue"
Invoke-Step "send prompt" {
  $sendScript = Join-Path $PSScriptRoot "send-wps-ai-prompt.ps1"
  $output = & $sendScript -TitleLike $wpsContext.name -PromptPath $PromptFullPath
  foreach ($line in $output) {
    Add-Log ("SEND_OUTPUT {0}" -f $line)
  }
```
</details></tool-use>

_**Agent (gpt-5.5 2026-04-25 08:26:46Z)**_

<tool-use data-tool-type="shell" data-tool-name="shell_command"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **shell_command** `rtk powershell -NoProfile -Command '$lines = Get-Content -LiteralPath "scripts/run-wps-ai-ui-test.ps1"; $lines[390..440]'`</summary>
```
Exit code: 0
Wall time: 1.2 seconds
Output:
  }
}

Set-TestStatus "Waiting for AI output in the WPS document..." "blue"
$monitorStart = Get-Date
$deadline = $monitorStart.AddSeconds($WaitSeconds)
$lastLength = -1
$stableRounds = 0
$contentStarted = $false

while ((Get-Date) -lt $deadline) {
  $text = Get-DocumentText $wpsContext.doc
  $length = $text.Length
  $tables = Get-TableSummary $wpsContext.doc

  if ($length -ne $lastLength) {
    Add-Log ("DOC_PROGRESS chars={0} tables={1}" -f $length, $tables.count)
    $lastLength = $length
    $stableRounds = 0
  } elseif ($length -gt 30) {
    $stableRounds++
  }

  if ($length -gt 30) {
    $contentStarted = $true
  }

  Set-TestStatus ("Waiting... chars={0}, tables={1}" -f $length, $tables.count) "blue"

  if ($contentStarted -and $stableRounds -ge 6) {
    break
  }

  Start-Sleep -Seconds 2
}

$finalText = Get-DocumentText $wpsContext.doc
$finalTables = Get-TableSummary $wpsContext.doc

if ($finalText.Length -le 30) {
  Save-Screenshot $wpsProcess $StuckShot
  Add-Log "NO_DOCUMENT_OUTPUT_AFTER_WAIT"
  Set-TestStatus "No document output detected; see log and screenshot." "red" $true
  throw "No WPS document output detected after waiting."
}

Set-TestStatus "Inspecting table, code highlight, headings, and body text..." "blue"
$formatStats = Get-FormatStats $wpsContext.doc
Save-Screenshot $wpsProcess $AfterShot

$tableOk = $false
```
</details></tool-use>

 function Wait-Taskpane {
-  param([System.Diagnostics.Process]$Process)
+  param(
+    [System.Diagnostics.Process]$Process,
+    [string]$DocumentName = ""
+  )
 
   $deadline = (Get-Date).AddSeconds(40)
+  $attempt = 0
   while ((Get-Date) -lt $deadline) {
-    $rect = Get-CefPaneRect $Process
-    if ($rect -and -not $rect.IsEmpty -and $rect.Width -gt 200 -and $rect.Height -gt 200) {
-      return $rect
+    $attempt++
+    $candidates = @()
+
+    if ($Process) {
+      $fresh = Get-Process -Id $Process.Id -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue
+      if ($fresh -and $fresh.MainWindowHandle -ne 0) {
+        $candidates += $fresh
+      }
+    }
+
+    if (-not [string]::IsNullOrWhiteSpace($DocumentName)) {
+      $named = Get-Process -Name wps -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue |
+        Where-Object {
+          $_.MainWindowHandle -ne 0 -and $_.MainWindowTitle -like "*$DocumentName*"
+        }
+      $candidates += $named
+    }
+
+    $allWps = Get-Process -Name wps -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue |
+      Where-Object {
+        $_.MainWindowHandle -ne 0 -and $_.MainWindowTitle -like "*WPS*"
+      }
+    $candidates += $allWps
+
+    $uniqueCandidates = $candidates |
+      Where-Object { $_ -and $_.MainWindowHandle -ne 0 } |
+      Sort-Object Id -Unique
+
+    foreach ($candidate in $uniqueCandidates) {
+      $rect = Get-CefPaneRect $candidate
+      if ($rect -and -not $rect.IsEmpty -and $rect.Width -gt 200 -and $rect.Height -gt 200) {
+        Add-Log ("TASKPANE_MATCH process={0} title={1} rect={2},{3},{4},{5}" -f $candidate.Id, $candidate.MainWindowTitle, [int]$rect.X, [int]$rect.Y, [int]$rect.Width, [int]$rect.Height)
+        return [pscustomobject]@{
+          process = $candidate
+          rect = $rect
+        }
+      }
+    }
+
+    if ($attempt -eq 1 -or $attempt % 5 -eq 0) {
+      $titles = ($uniqueCandidates | Select-Object -ExpandProperty MainWindowTitle) -join " | "
+      Add-Log ("TASKPANE_WAIT attempt={0} candidates={1} titles={2}" -f $attempt, ($uniqueCandidates | Measure-Object).Count, $titles)
     }
 
     Start-Sleep -Milliseconds 800
@@
 function Save-Screenshot {
@@
 }
+
+function Save-UiTree {
+  param(
+    [string]$TitleLike,
+    [string]$Path
+  )
+
+  $inspectScript = Join-Path $PSScriptRoot "inspect-wps-ui.ps1"
+  try {
+    $output = & $inspectScript -TitleLike $TitleLike -MaxDepth 14
+    [System.IO.File]::WriteAllLines($Path, [string[]]$output, $Utf8NoBom)
+    Add-Log ("UI_TREE {0}" -f $Path)
+  } catch {
+    Add-Log ("UI_TREE_FAILED {0}" -f $_.Exception.Message)
+  }
+}

 1 тФВ # RTK - Rust Token Killer (Codex CLI)
 2 тФВ 
 3 тФВ **Usage**: Token-optimized CLI proxy for shell commands.
 4 тФВ 
 5 тФВ ## Rule
 6 тФВ 
 7 тФВ Always prefix shell commands with `rtk`.
 8 тФВ 
 9 тФВ Examples:
10 тФВ 
11 тФВ ```bash
12 тФВ rtk git status
13 тФВ rtk cargo test
14 тФВ rtk npm run build
15 тФВ rtk pytest -q
16 тФВ ```
17 тФВ 
18 тФВ ## Meta Commands
19 тФВ 
20 тФВ ```bash
21 тФВ rtk gain            # Token savings analytics
22 тФВ rtk gain --history  # Recent command savings history
23 тФВ rtk proxy <cmd>     # Run raw command without filtering
24 тФВ ```
25 тФВ 
26 тФВ ## Verification
27 тФВ 
28 тФВ ```bash
29 тФВ rtk --version
30 тФВ rtk gain
31 тФВ which rtk
32 тФВ ```
```

  1 тФВ ---
  2 тФВ name: windows-powershell
  3 тФВ description: Use when composing or running Windows PowerShell commands from Codex, especially through `rtk powershell`, `powershell -Command`, `powershell -File`, commands with `$_`, pipes, quotes, paths with spaces, environment variables, or scripts. Prevents repeated quoting, escaping, and script invocation mistakes on Windows.
  4 тФВ ---
  5 тФВ 
  6 тФВ # Windows PowerShell
  7 тФВ 
  8 тФВ Use this skill before running non-trivial PowerShell commands on Windows. Keep updates concise: add only correct reusable command forms and decision rules, not failing commands or incident notes.
  9 тФВ 
 10 тФВ ## Command Review
 11 тФВ 
 12 тФВ Before executing, check:
 13 тФВ 
 14 тФВ - Entry mode: `rtk powershell -Command` for inline script, `rtk powershell -File` only for `.ps1`.
 15 тФВ - Write scope: target files, install directories, generated artifacts, processes.
 16 тФВ - Fragile tokens: `$_`, `$env:...`, `|`, `>`, `<`, `&`, `;`, quotes, paths with spaces.
 17 тФВ - Whether a small read-only probe should run before a write/copy/remove/build/install command.
 18 тФВ 
 19 тФВ ## Entry Rules
 20 тФВ 
 21 тФВ - Run PowerShell scripts as:
 22 тФВ 
 23 тФВ ```powershell
 24 тФВ rtk powershell -NoProfile -ExecutionPolicy Bypass -File ".\scripts\task.ps1"
 25 тФВ ```
 26 тФВ 
 27 тФВ - Run `.py`, `.js`, `.mjs`, `.bat`, or `.cmd` with their own executable, not `powershell -File`.
 28 тФВ - Use `rtk <program> ...` directly when PowerShell is unnecessary, for example `rtk git status`, `rtk npm test`, `rtk node script.mjs`.
 29 тФВ - For `.ps1` scripts that may run under Windows PowerShell 5, keep parameter defaults and control strings ASCII, or save explicitly as UTF-8 with BOM; otherwise non-ASCII defaults can be decoded incorrectly.
 30 тФВ - For UTF-8 prompt or fixture files that may contain Chinese, read with `.NET` encoding APIs: `[System.IO.File]::ReadAllText($path, [System.Text.UTF8Encoding]::new($false, $true))`.
 31 тФВ - In PowerShell functions that return objects, do not use `Write-Output` for logging; it pollutes the success pipeline. Log to a file and use `Write-Host` only for human-visible progress.
 32 тФВ 
 33 тФВ ## Quoting Rules
 34 тФВ 
 35 тФВ Prefer outer single quotes when inline PowerShell contains `$`, `$env:...`, or `$_`:
 36 тФВ 
 37 тФВ ```powershell
 38 тФВ rtk powershell -NoProfile -Command 'Get-Process | Where-Object { $_.ProcessName -match "node|vite|wps" } | Select-Object Id,ProcessName'
 39 тФВ ```
 40 тФВ 
 41 тФВ ```powershell
 42 тФВ rtk powershell -NoProfile -Command '$p = Join-Path $env:APPDATA "kingsoft\wps"; Get-ChildItem -LiteralPath $p'
 43 тФВ ```
 44 тФВ 
 45 тФВ When outer double quotes are required by the caller, escape every PowerShell `$` as backtick-dollar:
 46 тФВ 
 47 тФВ ```powershell
 48 тФВ rtk powershell -NoProfile -Command "`$target = Join-Path `$env:APPDATA 'kingsoft\wps\jsaddons'; Get-ChildItem -LiteralPath `$target -Directory | Select-Object Name,LastWriteTime"
 49 тФВ ```
 50 тФВ 
 51 тФВ For `$_` with outer double quotes, escape `$_` and quote regex alternation inside the script:
 52 тФВ 
 53 тФВ ```powershell
 54 тФВ rtk powershell -NoProfile -Command "Get-Process | Where-Object { `$_.ProcessName -match 'node|vite|wps|powershell' } | Select-Object Id,ProcessName,StartTime"
 55 тФВ ```
 56 тФВ 
 57 тФВ For nested quotes in WMI/CIM filters, assign the filter to a variable first:
 58 тФВ 
 59 тФВ ```powershell
 60 тФВ rtk powershell -NoProfile -Command "`$filter = 'Name = ''powershell.exe'''; Get-CimInstance Win32_Process -Filter `$filter | Select-Object ProcessId,CommandLine"
 61 тФВ ```
 62 тФВ 
 63 тФВ ## Paths And Files
 64 тФВ 
 65 тФВ - Use `-LiteralPath` for exact paths, especially paths with spaces, brackets, wildcards, or non-ASCII characters.
 66 тФВ - Quote all Windows paths.
 67 тФВ - Prefer `Join-Path` for paths built from `$env:APPDATA`, `$HOME`, or workspace variables.
 68 тФВ 
 69 тФВ Read a file:
 70 тФВ 
 71 тФВ ```powershell
 72 тФВ rtk powershell -NoProfile -Command 'Get-Content -LiteralPath "C:\path\file.txt"'
 73 тФВ ```
 74 тФВ 
 75 тФВ List selected files:
 76 тФВ 
 77 тФВ ```powershell
 78 тФВ rtk powershell -NoProfile -Command 'Get-ChildItem -LiteralPath "C:\path" -Force | Select-Object Name,LastWriteTime,Length'
 79 тФВ ```
 80 тФВ 
 81 тФВ Inspect generated or installed artifacts under `%APPDATA%`:
 82 тФВ 
 83 тФВ ```powershell
 84 тФВ rtk powershell -NoProfile -Command "`$asset = Join-Path `$env:APPDATA 'kingsoft\wps\jsaddons\wps-ai_1.0.4\assets'; Get-ChildItem -LiteralPath `$asset -Filter 'taskpane-*.js' | Select-Object Name,LastWriteTime,Length"
 85 тФВ ```
 86 тФВ 
 87 тФВ For timestamp checks where the target directory is already specific, list and sort the target directly instead of embedding complex regex filters:
 88 тФВ 
 89 тФВ ```powershell
 90 тФВ rtk powershell -NoProfile -Command '$target = Join-Path $env:APPDATA "kingsof
... (truncated)
```
</details></tool-use>

 1 тФВ ---
 2 тФВ name: wps-ai-testing
 3 тФВ description: Use when running or troubleshooting WPS-AI-CHAT desktop UI tests in WPS Writer, especially tests that must send a prompt through the WPS AI taskpane and verify WPS document rendering for headings, body text, tables, and code highlighting.
 4 тФВ ---
 5 тФВ 
 6 тФВ # WPS AI Testing
 7 тФВ 
 8 тФВ Use the project test runner instead of ad hoc click commands.
 9 тФВ 
10 тФВ ## Entry Point
11 тФВ 
12 тФВ From `d:\cursor_dev\wps_ai`:
13 тФВ 
14 тФВ ```powershell
15 тФВ rtk powershell -NoProfile -ExecutionPolicy Bypass -File ".\scripts\run-wps-ai-ui-test.ps1"
16 тФВ ```
17 тФВ 
18 тФВ Options:
19 тФВ 
20 тФВ ```powershell
21 тФВ rtk powershell -NoProfile -ExecutionPolicy Bypass -File ".\scripts\run-wps-ai-ui-test.ps1" -WaitSeconds 240
22 тФВ rtk powershell -NoProfile -ExecutionPolicy Bypass -File ".\scripts\run-wps-ai-ui-test.ps1" -NoPopup
23 тФВ ```
24 тФВ 
25 тФВ ## Rules
26 тФВ 
27 тФВ 1. Do not start with loose `click-screen` commands. Run the runner first so every step is logged.
28 тФВ 2. The runner shows a colored topmost status popup and writes logs under `test-artifacts\wps-ai-ui\<timestamp>\`.
29 тФВ 3. The fixed prompt is `scripts\wps-ai-render-test-prompt.txt`; read prompt files with .NET UTF-8 APIs, not plain `Get-Content`, when clipboard text may contain Chinese.
30 тФВ 4. Use the WPS taskpane `CefBrowserWindow` rectangle to derive input/send coordinates.
31 тФВ 5. If output does not appear before timeout, stop and inspect `run.log`, `summary.json`, and `stuck.png`; do not repeat the same send command.
32 тФВ 6. Leave the generated WPS document open for manual inspection.
33 тФВ 
34 тФВ ## Validation Targets
35 тФВ 
36 тФВ The test must check:
37 тФВ 
38 тФВ - AI taskpane prompt submission.
39 тФВ - WPS body text output length.
40 тФВ - At least one real WPS table with expected dimensions.
41 тФВ - Code font and colored code characters.
42 тФВ - Heading-like paragraphs, bold, and italic formatting.
43 тФВ - Screenshots before send and after output.
```
</details></tool-use>

_**Agent (gpt-5.5 2026-04-25 08:32:32Z)**_

<tool-use data-tool-type="shell" data-tool-name="shell_command"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **shell_command** `rtk read -n --max-lines 260 C:\Users\10297441.WIN-9DOP5T7GHM7\.codex\skills\rtk-usage\SKILL.md`</summary>
```
Exit code: 0

Output:
  1 тФВ ---
  2 тФВ name: rtk-usage
  3 тФВ description: Use when working with `rtk` on Windows or Codex, especially for command selection, validating `rtk` behavior, fixing `rtk` failures, diagnosing `No hook installed`, or avoiding incorrect fallback to raw shell commands. Trigger for `rtk` installation, upgrade, init, hook, rewrite, proxy, env, json, verify, gain, and command-usage troubleshooting.
  4 тФВ ---
  5 тФВ 
  6 тФВ # RTK Usage
  7 тФВ 
  8 тФВ Use this skill whenever a task depends on `rtk` working correctly.
  9 тФВ 
 10 тФВ ## Core Rule
 11 тФВ 
 12 тФВ 1. If an `rtk` command fails, do not fall back to the equivalent raw shell command.
 13 тФВ 2. Diagnose and fix the `rtk` issue first.
 14 тФВ 3. Only continue the main task after `rtk` is working again.
 15 тФВ 4. Prefer RTK-native subcommands over `rtk run` / `rtk proxy` when a native subcommand exists.
 16 тФВ 5. On Windows, quote carefully in the outer shell; many apparent RTK failures are PowerShell quoting errors.
 17 тФВ 6. If a non-interactive command can be run through `rtk`, run it through `rtk`.
 18 тФВ 7. Raw shell is allowed only when exact byte-for-byte output matters, the command is tiny/trivial, the command is interactive, or `rtk` would change semantics.
 19 тФВ 8. When updating this skill, add only correct reusable RTK command forms and decision rules.
 20 тФВ 
 21 тФВ ## Windows Baseline Checks
 22 тФВ 
 23 тФВ 1. Run `rtk --version`.
 24 тФВ 2. Run `rtk --help`.
 25 тФВ 3. Run `rtk gain`.
 26 тФВ 4. Run `rtk init -g --show` for Claude hook status.
 27 тФВ 5. Run `rtk init -g --codex --show` for Codex global status.
 28 тФВ 6. Run `rtk verify`.
 29 тФВ 7. Run `rtk proxy where.exe rtk` to confirm the active binary.
 30 тФВ 8. On Windows release installs, prefer the official GitHub asset `rtk-x86_64-pc-windows-msvc.zip`.
 31 тФВ 
 32 тФВ Known-good Windows baseline observed for `rtk 0.37.2`:
 33 тФВ 
 34 тФВ ```bash
 35 тФВ rtk --version
 36 тФВ rtk --help
 37 тФВ rtk gain
 38 тФВ rtk verify
 39 тФВ rtk init -g --show
 40 тФВ rtk init -g --codex --show
 41 тФВ rtk proxy where.exe rtk
 42 тФВ ```
 43 тФВ 
 44 тФВ ## Correct Command Patterns
 45 тФВ 
 46 тФВ Use these forms exactly:
 47 тФВ 
 48 тФВ ```bash
 49 тФВ rtk env --filter PATH
 50 тФВ rtk json package.json
 51 тФВ rtk rewrite "git status --short"
 52 тФВ rtk proxy git status --short
 53 тФВ rtk proxy powershell -NoProfile -Command "Get-Content package.json -TotalCount 20"
 54 тФВ rtk powershell -NoProfile -Command "Get-Content package.json -TotalCount 20"
 55 тФВ rtk run "git branch --show-current"
 56 тФВ rtk run -c "git status --short"
 57 тФВ rtk verify
 58 тФВ rtk help hook
 59 тФВ rtk hook check git status --short
 60 тФВ ```
 61 тФВ 
 62 тФВ Rules:
 63 тФВ 
 64 тФВ 1. `rtk env` uses `--filter`; do not pass variable names as positional arguments.
 65 тФВ 2. `rtk json` takes a JSON file path; do not pass inline JSON text.
 66 тФВ 3. `rtk rewrite` can exit non-zero even after printing a valid rewrite in some Windows builds; judge by printed output and `rtk rewrite --help`, not by code alone.
 67 тФВ 4. `rtk proxy` is acceptable for diagnosis because it still goes through `rtk`.
 68 тФВ 5. `rtk run` uses shell execution semantics; prefer RTK-native commands first, then `rtk proxy` when quoting is simpler.
 69 тФВ 6. Do not use `rtk read --line` or `--line-count`; current `rtk read` supports `--max-lines`, `--tail-lines`, and `--line-numbers`.
 70 тФВ 7. `rtk powershell ...` is valid when PowerShell is the intended program; `rtk proxy powershell ...` is also acceptable for diagnosis.
 71 тФВ 8. On this Windows environment, use `powershell`, not `pwsh`, unless `rtk proxy where.exe pwsh` confirms PowerShell 7 is installed.
 72 тФВ 
 73 тФВ ## Correct Windows Recipes
 74 тФВ 
 75 тФВ Use these positive patterns for recurring Windows command scenarios:
 76 тФВ 
 77 тФВ 1. PowerShell on this machine: use `rtk powershell ...` by default; only use `pwsh` after `rtk proxy where.exe pwsh` confirms it exists.
 78 тФВ 2. Inline PowerShell containing `$`, `$env:...`, or `$_`: use outer single quotes around the PowerShell script, or escape each `$` as `` `$ `` when outer double quotes are unavoidable.
 79 тФВ 3. PowerShell regex alternation: quote the regex inside the PowerShell script, for example `-match "node|vite|wps"`.
 80 тФВ 4. Python scripts: run with `rtk python script.py`; do not use PowerShell as the interpreter.
 81 тФВ 5. Node scripts: run with `rtk node script.mjs` or the project package wrapper, not through PowerShell unless shell features are needed.
 82 тФВ 6. PowerShell scripts: run with `rtk powershell -NoProfile -ExecutionPolicy Bypass -File .\script.ps1`.
 83 тФВ 7. Builds with post-build installers: use the project wrapper through `rtk`, then verify generated artifact timestamps and install directories if the command takes longer than expected.
 84 тФВ 8. Command diagnosis: classify failures as RTK resolution, Pow
... (truncated)
```

)

$ErrorActionPreference = "Stop"

Add-Type -AssemblyName UIAutomationClient
Add-Type -AssemblyName UIAutomationTypes

$Utf8NoBom = [System.Text.UTF8Encoding]::new($false)
$ProjectRoot = (Resolve-Path -LiteralPath (Join-Path $PSScriptRoot "..")).Path
$PromptFullPath = if ([System.IO.Path]::IsPathRooted($PromptPath)) {
  (Resolve-Path -LiteralPath $PromptPath).Path
} else {
  (Resolve-Path -LiteralPath (Join-Path $ProjectRoot $PromptPath)).Path
}

$LogRootFullPath = if ([System.IO.Path]::IsPathRooted($LogRoot)) {
  $LogRoot
} else {
  Join-Path $ProjectRoot $LogRoot
}

$RunId = Get-Date -Format "yyyyMMdd-HHmmss"
$RunDir = Join-Path $LogRootFullPath $RunId
New-Item -ItemType Directory -Path $RunDir -Force | Out-Null

$LogPath = Join-Path $RunDir "run.log"
$StatusPath = Join-Path $RunDir "status.json"
$SummaryPath = Join-Path $RunDir "summary.json"
$BeforeShot = Join-Path $RunDir "before-send.png"
$AfterShot = Join-Path $RunDir "after-output.png"
$StuckShot = Join-Path $RunDir "stuck.png"
$TaskpaneFailTree = Join-Path $RunDir "taskpane-failed-ui-tree.txt"

function Add-Log {
  param([string]$Message)
  $line = "[{0}] {1}" -f (Get-Date -Format "yyyy-MM-dd HH:mm:ss.fff"), $Message
  [System.IO.File]::AppendAllText($LogPath, $line + [Environment]::NewLine, $Utf8NoBom)
  Write-Host $line
}

function Set-TestStatus {
  param(
    [string]$Message,
    [string]$Color = "blue",
    [bool]$Done = $false
  )

  $status = [pscustomobject]@{
    title = "WPS AI UI Test"
    message = $Message
    color = $Color
    done = $Done
    time = (Get-Date).ToString("s")
    log = $LogPath
  }

  [System.IO.File]::WriteAllText(
    $StatusPath,
    ($status | ConvertTo-Json -Compress),
    $Utf8NoBom
  )

  Add-Log ("STATUS {0}: {1}" -f $Color, $Message)
}

function Start-StatusPopup {
  if ($NoPopup) {
    return
  }

  $scriptPath = Join-Path $PSScriptRoot "show-wps-test-status.ps1"
  Start-Process -FilePath "powershell" -WindowStyle Hidden -ArgumentList @(
    "-NoProfile",
    "-ExecutionPolicy",
    "Bypass",

    $wpsProcess = Get-Process -Name wps -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue |

function Get-CefPaneRect {
  param([System.Diagnostics.Process]$Process)

  if (-not $Process -or $Process.MainWindowHandle -eq 0) {
    return $null
  }

  $root = [System.Windows.Automation.AutomationElement]::FromHandle($Process.MainWindowHandle)
  if (-not $root) {
    return $null
  }

  $cefPane = $root.FindFirst(
    [System.Windows.Automation.TreeScope]::Descendants,
    [System.Windows.Automation.PropertyCondition]::new(
      [System.Windows.Automation.AutomationElement]::ClassNameProperty,
      "CefBrowserWindow"
    )
  )

  if ($cefPane) {
    return $cefPane.Current.BoundingRectangle
  }

  $taskpane = $root.FindFirst(
    [System.Windows.Automation.TreeScope]::Descendants,
    [System.Windows.Automation.PropertyCondition]::new(
      [System.Windows.Automation.AutomationElement]::ClassNameProperty,
      "KxJSCTPWidget"
    )
  )

  if ($taskpane) {
    return $taskpane.Current.BoundingRectangle
  }

  return $null
}

@@
 const STORAGE_KEY = "wps_ai_taskpane_id";
+const AUTO_OPEN_RETRY_LIMIT = 30;
+const AUTO_OPEN_RETRY_MS = 500;
+let autoOpenTimerId = 0;
+let autoOpenAttempts = 0;
@@
 function ensureTaskPane() {
@@
   return taskPane;
 }
+
+function showTaskPaneIfDocumentReady() {
+  if (!window.Application?.ActiveDocument) {
+    return false;
+  }
+
+  const taskPane = ensureTaskPane();
+  taskPane.Visible = true;
+  return true;
+}
+
+function scheduleAutoOpenTaskPane() {
+  if (autoOpenTimerId) {
+    window.clearTimeout(autoOpenTimerId);
+    autoOpenTimerId = 0;
+  }
+
+  autoOpenAttempts = 0;
+
+  function tryAutoOpen() {
+    autoOpenAttempts += 1;
+
+    try {
+      if (showTaskPaneIfDocumentReady()) {
+        autoOpenTimerId = 0;
+        return;
+      }
+    } catch {
+      // WPS may expose Application before ActiveDocument is ready.
+    }
+
+    if (autoOpenAttempts < AUTO_OPEN_RETRY_LIMIT) {
+      autoOpenTimerId = window.setTimeout(tryAutoOpen, AUTO_OPEN_RETRY_MS);
+    } else {
+      autoOpenTimerId = 0;
+    }

@@
   [string]$PromptPath = ".\scripts\wps-ai-render-test-prompt.txt",

@@
 $RunId = Get-Date -Format "yyyyMMdd-HHmmss"
 $RunDir = Join-Path $LogRootFullPath $RunId
 New-Item -ItemType Directory -Path $RunDir -Force | Out-Null
+$CaptureDir = Join-Path $RunDir "captures"
+New-Item -ItemType Directory -Path $CaptureDir -Force | Out-Null
 
 $LogPath = Join-Path $RunDir "run.log"
@@
 function Invoke-Step {
@@
 }
+
+function Close-ExistingWpsWriter {
+  $processes = @(Get-Process -Name wps -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue)
+  if ($processes.Count -eq 0) {
+    Add-Log "CLOSE_WPS none"
+    return
+  }
+
+  foreach ($process in $processes) {
+    Add-Log ("CLOSE_WPS request id={0} title={1}" -f $process.Id, $process.MainWindowTitle)
+    try {
+      if ($process.MainWindowHandle -ne 0) {
+        $null = $process.CloseMainWindow()
+      } else {
+        Stop-Process -Id $process.Id -Force -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue
+      }
+    } catch {
+      Add-Log ("CLOSE_WPS request_failed id={0} error={1}" -f $process.Id, $_.Exception.Message)
+    }
+  }
+
+  $deadline = (Get-Date).AddSeconds(12)
+  while ((Get-Date) -lt $deadline) {
+    $remaining = @(Get-Process -Name wps -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue)
+    if ($remaining.Count -eq 0) {
+      Add-Log "CLOSE_WPS closed"
+      return
+    }
+
+    Start-Sleep -Milliseconds 500
+  }
+
+  $remaining = @(Get-Process -Name wps -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue)
+  foreach ($process in $remaining) {
+    Add-Log ("CLOSE_WPS force id={0} title={1}" -f $process.Id, $process.MainWindowTitle)
+    Stop-Process -Id $process.Id -Force -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue
+  }
+}
@@
-function Get-CefPaneRect {
+function Find-DescendantByClass {
+  param(
+    [System.Windows.Automation.AutomationElement]$Element,
+    [string[]]$ClassNames,
+    [int]$MaxDepth = 24,
+    [int]$Depth = 0
+  )
+
+  if (-not $Element -or $Depth -gt $MaxDepth) {
+    return $null
+  }
+
+  try {
+    $className = [string]$Element.Current.ClassName
+    if ($ClassNames -contains $className) {
+      return $Element
+    }
+  } catch {}
+
+  try {
+    $children = $Element.FindAll(
+      [System.Windows.Automation.TreeScope]::Children,
+      [System.Windows.Automation.Condition]::TrueCondition
+    )
+  } catch {
+    return $null
+  }
+
+  foreach ($child in $children) {
+    $found = Find-DescendantByClass $child $ClassNames $MaxDepth ($Depth + 1)
+    if ($found) {
+      return $found
+    }
+  }
+
+  return $null
+}

@@
 function Save-Screenshot {
@@
   Add-Log ("SCREENSHOT {0}" -f $Path)
 }
+
+function Save-CaptureFrame {
+  param(
+    [System.Diagnostics.Process]$Process,
+    [string]$Stage
+  )
+
+  if (-not $Process -or $Process.MainWindowHandle -eq 0) {
+    return
+  }
+
+  $stamp = Get-Date -Format "HHmmss-fff"
+  $safeStage = ($Stage -replace "[^A-Za-z0-9_-]", "-")
+  $path = Join-Path $CaptureDir ("{0}-{1}.png" -f $stamp, $safeStage)
+  try {
+    Save-Screenshot $Process $path
+  } catch {
+    Add-Log ("CAPTURE_FAILED stage={0} error={1}" -f $Stage, $_.Exception.Message)
+  }
+}

_**Agent (gpt-5.5 2026-04-25 08:35:51Z)**_

<tool-use data-tool-type="write" data-tool-name="apply_patch"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **apply_patch**</summary>
**Update: `scripts/README-wps-ai-ui-test.md`**

```diff
@@
 The runner creates a timestamped folder under `test-artifacts/wps-ai-ui/` with:
@@
 - `before-send.png`, `after-output.png`, and `stuck.png` when applicable.
+- `captures/`: periodic screenshots while waiting for the taskpane and generated document output.
@@
 - Do not send ad hoc click commands first. Run the test runner so progress and failures are logged.
+- The runner closes existing WPS Writer (`wps.exe`) windows before each run by default; it does not close WPS Spreadsheets (`et.exe`).
 - Keep `.ps1` defaults ASCII. Read prompt files with .NET UTF-8 APIs.
-- Use the taskpane `CefBrowserWindow` rectangle to derive click coordinates.
+- Use recursive UI Automation traversal to find `CefBrowserWindow` or `KxJSCTPWidget`; single-call `FindFirst` can miss nested WPS/CEF panes.
 - If output does not appear before timeout, stop and inspect the log/screenshot instead of repeating the same send command.
 - Leave the WPS document open after the run for manual inspection.
```
</details></tool-use>

_**Agent (gpt-5.5 2026-04-25 08:36:13Z)**_

<tool-use data-tool-type="write" data-tool-name="apply_patch"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **apply_patch**</summary>
**Update: `C:/Users/10297441.WIN-9DOP5T7GHM7/.codex/skills/wps-ai-testing/SKILL.md`**

```diff
@@
 Options:
 
 ```powershell
 rtk powershell -NoProfile -ExecutionPolicy Bypass -File ".\scripts\run-wps-ai-ui-test.ps1" -WaitSeconds 240
 rtk powershell -NoProfile -ExecutionPolicy Bypass -File ".\scripts\run-wps-ai-ui-test.ps1" -NoPopup
+rtk powershell -NoProfile -ExecutionPolicy Bypass -File ".\scripts\run-wps-ai-ui-test.ps1" -KeepExistingWps
 ```
@@
 2. The runner shows a colored topmost status popup and writes logs under `test-artifacts\wps-ai-ui\<timestamp>\`.
-3. The fixed prompt is `scripts\wps-ai-render-test-prompt.txt`; read prompt files with .NET UTF-8 APIs, not plain `Get-Content`, when clipboard text may contain Chinese.
-4. Use the WPS taskpane `CefBrowserWindow` rectangle to derive input/send coordinates.
-5. If output does not appear before timeout, stop and inspect `run.log`, `summary.json`, and `stuck.png`; do not repeat the same send command.
-6. Leave the generated WPS document open for manual inspection.
+3. By default the runner closes existing WPS Writer (`wps.exe`) windows before opening a fresh document; it must not close WPS Spreadsheets (`et.exe`).
+4. The runner writes periodic screenshots under `captures\` while waiting for the taskpane and document output.
+5. The fixed prompt is `scripts\wps-ai-render-test-prompt.txt`; read prompt files with .NET UTF-8 APIs, not plain `Get-Content`, when clipboard text may contain Chinese.
+6. Use recursive UI Automation traversal to find `CefBrowserWindow` or `KxJSCTPWidget`; single-call `FindFirst` can miss nested WPS/CEF panes.
+7. If output does not appear before timeout, stop and inspect `run.log`, `summary.json`, `captures\`, and `stuck.png`; do not repeat the same send command.
+8. Leave the generated WPS document open for manual inspection.
```
</details></tool-use>

<tool-use data-tool-type="write" data-tool-name="apply_patch"><details>

```diff
@@
 For WPS-AI-CHAT desktop UI tests, use the project runner instead of loose click commands:
 
 ```powershell
 rtk powershell -NoProfile -ExecutionPolicy Bypass -File ".\scripts\run-wps-ai-ui-test.ps1"
 ```
+
+WPS-AI-CHAT test runner rules:
+
+- The runner closes existing WPS Writer (`wps.exe`) windows by default and must not close WPS Spreadsheets (`et.exe`).
+- Use recursive UI Automation traversal for nested WPS/CEF panes; detect either `CefBrowserWindow` or `KxJSCTPWidget`.
+- Keep periodic screenshots under the run directory `captures\` while waiting for UI or document output.
```
</details></tool-use>

  8 тФВ function readLocalSession(document) {

 10 тФВ

 14 тФВ       return {
 15 тФВ         exists: false,
 16 тФВ         messages: []
 17 тФВ       };
 18 тФВ     }

 20 тФВ     const parsed = JSON.parse(raw);

 22 тФВ       exists: true,

 24 тФВ     };

    return "";

  const range = getSelectionRange(app);
  return range?.Text ? String(range.Text) : "";
}

export function readDocumentInfo() {
  const app = getApplication();
  const doc = app?.ActiveDocument;
  if (!doc) {
    return null;
  }

  const title = String(doc.Name ?? "").trim() || "Untitled Document";
  const path = String(doc.Path ?? "").trim();
  let fullName = String(doc.FullName ?? "").trim();

  if (!fullName && path) {
    fullName = `${path}${path.endsWith("\\") || path.endsWith("/") ? "" : "\\"}${title}`;
  }

  const normalizedFullName = fullName.trim();
  return {
    key: normalizedFullName
      ? `saved:${normalizedFullName.toLowerCase()}`
      : `unsaved:${title}`,
    title,
    name: title,
    path,
    fullName: normalizedFullName,
    isSaved: Boolean(normalizedFullName)
  };
}

export function hasWpsDocument() {
  const app = getApplication();
  return Boolean(app?.ActiveDocument);
}

export function createWpsMarkdownSink({ replaceSelection } = {}) {
```

_**Agent (gpt-5.5 2026-04-25 08:46:41Z)**_

<tool-use data-tool-type="write" data-tool-name="apply_patch"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **apply_patch**</summary>
**Update: `scripts/README-wps-ai-ui-test.md`**

```diff
@@
 - The runner closes existing WPS Writer (`wps.exe`) windows before each run by default; it does not close WPS Spreadsheets (`et.exe`).
+- The runner saves each test document as a unique ASCII `.docx` under the run folder to avoid reusing stale `цЦЗхнЧцЦЗчи┐1` sessions.
 - Keep `.ps1` defaults ASCII. Read prompt files with .NET UTF-8 APIs.
 - Use recursive UI Automation traversal to find `CefBrowserWindow` or `KxJSCTPWidget`; single-call `FindFirst` can miss nested WPS/CEF panes.
+- Submit prompts by focusing the textarea, pressing `Ctrl+A`, pasting UTF-8 clipboard text, and pressing `Enter`; use button clicks only as a fallback.
```
</details></tool-use>

```diff
@@
 3. By default the runner closes existing WPS Writer (`wps.exe`) windows before opening a fresh document; it must not close WPS Spreadsheets (`et.exe`).
 4. The runner writes periodic screenshots under `captures\` while waiting for the taskpane and document output.
-5. The fixed prompt is `scripts\wps-ai-render-test-prompt.txt`; read prompt files with .NET UTF-8 APIs, not plain `Get-Content`, when clipboard text may contain Chinese.
-6. Use recursive UI Automation traversal to find `CefBrowserWindow` or `KxJSCTPWidget`; single-call `FindFirst` can miss nested WPS/CEF panes.
-7. If output does not appear before timeout, stop and inspect `run.log`, `summary.json`, `captures\`, and `stuck.png`; do not repeat the same send command.
-8. Leave the generated WPS document open for manual inspection.
+5. Save each test document as a unique ASCII `.docx` in the run folder before sending the prompt; this avoids stale `цЦЗхнЧцЦЗчи┐1` conversation history.
+6. The fixed prompt is `scripts\wps-ai-render-test-prompt.txt`; read prompt files with .NET UTF-8 APIs, not plain `Get-Content`, when clipboard text may contain Chinese.
+7. Use recursive UI Automation traversal to find `CefBrowserWindow` or `KxJSCTPWidget`; single-call `FindFirst` can miss nested WPS/CEF panes.
+8. Submit prompts by focusing the textarea, pressing `Ctrl+A`, pasting UTF-8 clipboard text, and pressing `Enter`; use button clicks only as a fallback.
+9. If output does not appear before timeout, stop and inspect `run.log`, `summary.json`, `captures\`, and `stuck.png`; do not repeat the same send command.
+10. Leave the generated WPS document open for manual inspection.
```
</details></tool-use>

_**Agent (gpt-5.5 2026-04-25 08:47:17Z)**_

<tool-use data-tool-type="write" data-tool-name="apply_patch"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **apply_patch**</summary>
**Update: `C:/Users/10297441.WIN-9DOP5T7GHM7/.codex/skills/windows-powershell/SKILL.md`**

```diff
@@
 - The runner closes existing WPS Writer (`wps.exe`) windows by default and must not close WPS Spreadsheets (`et.exe`).
 - Use recursive UI Automation traversal for nested WPS/CEF panes; detect either `CefBrowserWindow` or `KxJSCTPWidget`.
 - Keep periodic screenshots under the run directory `captures\` while waiting for UI or document output.
+- Save temporary WPS test documents with unique ASCII file names before sending prompts, so session lookup does not reuse stale unsaved `цЦЗхнЧцЦЗчи┐1` history.
+- For taskpane prompt submission, focus the textarea, press `Ctrl+A`, paste, then press `Enter`; button-coordinate clicks are only a fallback.
```
</details></tool-use>

 1 тФВ # RTK - Rust Token Killer (Codex CLI)
 2 тФВ 
 3 тФВ **Usage**: Token-optimized CLI proxy for shell commands.
 4 тФВ 
 5 тФВ ## Rule
 6 тФВ 
 7 тФВ Always prefix shell commands with `rtk`.
 8 тФВ 
 9 тФВ Examples:
10 тФВ 
11 тФВ ```bash
12 тФВ rtk git status
13 тФВ rtk cargo test
14 тФВ rtk npm run build
15 тФВ rtk pytest -q
16 тФВ ```
17 тФВ 
18 тФВ ## Meta Commands
19 тФВ 
20 тФВ ```bash
21 тФВ rtk gain            # Token savings analytics
22 тФВ rtk gain --history  # Recent command savings history
23 тФВ rtk proxy <cmd>     # Run raw command without filtering
24 тФВ ```
25 тФВ 
26 тФВ ## Verification
27 тФВ 
28 тФВ ```bash
29 тФВ rtk --version
30 тФВ rtk gain
31 тФВ which rtk
32 тФВ ```

  1 тФВ ---
  2 тФВ name: windows-powershell
  3 тФВ description: Use when composing or running Windows PowerShell commands from Codex, especially through `rtk powershell`, `powershell -Command`, `powershell -File`, commands with `$_`, pipes, quotes, paths with spaces, environment variables, or scripts. Prevents repeated quoting, escaping, and script invocation mistakes on Windows.
  4 тФВ ---
  5 тФВ 
  6 тФВ # Windows PowerShell
  7 тФВ 
  8 тФВ Use this skill before running non-trivial PowerShell commands on Windows. Keep updates concise: add only correct reusable command forms and decision rules, not failing commands or incident notes.
  9 тФВ 
 10 тФВ ## Command Review
 11 тФВ 
 12 тФВ Before executing, check:
 13 тФВ 
 14 тФВ - Entry mode: `rtk powershell -Command` for inline script, `rtk powershell -File` only for `.ps1`.
 15 тФВ - Write scope: target files, install directories, generated artifacts, processes.
 16 тФВ - Fragile tokens: `$_`, `$env:...`, `|`, `>`, `<`, `&`, `;`, quotes, paths with spaces.
 17 тФВ - Whether a small read-only probe should run before a write/copy/remove/build/install command.
 18 тФВ 
 19 тФВ ## Entry Rules
 20 тФВ 
 21 тФВ - Run PowerShell scripts as:
 22 тФВ 
 23 тФВ ```powershell
 24 тФВ rtk powershell -NoProfile -ExecutionPolicy Bypass -File ".\scripts\task.ps1"
 25 тФВ ```
 26 тФВ 
 27 тФВ - Run `.py`, `.js`, `.mjs`, `.bat`, or `.cmd` with their own executable, not `powershell -File`.
 28 тФВ - Use `rtk <program> ...` directly when PowerShell is unnecessary, for example `rtk git status`, `rtk npm test`, `rtk node script.mjs`.
 29 тФВ - For `.ps1` scripts that may run under Windows PowerShell 5, keep parameter defaults and control strings ASCII, or save explicitly as UTF-8 with BOM; otherwise non-ASCII defaults can be decoded incorrectly.
 30 тФВ - For UTF-8 prompt or fixture files that may contain Chinese, read with `.NET` encoding APIs: `[System.IO.File]::ReadAllText($path, [System.Text.UTF8Encoding]::new($false, $true))`.
 31 тФВ - In PowerShell functions that return objects, do not use `Write-Output` for logging; it pollutes the success pipeline. Log to a file and use `Write-Host` only for human-visible progress.
 32 тФВ 
 33 тФВ ## Quoting Rules
 34 тФВ 
 35 тФВ Prefer outer single quotes when inline PowerShell contains `$`, `$env:...`, or `$_`:
 36 тФВ 
 37 тФВ ```powershell
 38 тФВ rtk powershell -NoProfile -Command 'Get-Process | Where-Object { $_.ProcessName -match "node|vite|wps" } | Select-Object Id,ProcessName'
 39 тФВ ```
 40 тФВ 
 41 тФВ ```powershell
 42 тФВ rtk powershell -NoProfile -Command '$p = Join-Path $env:APPDATA "kingsoft\wps"; Get-ChildItem -LiteralPath $p'
 43 тФВ ```
 44 тФВ 
 45 тФВ When outer double quotes are required by the caller, escape every PowerShell `$` as backtick-dollar:
 46 тФВ 
 47 тФВ ```powershell
 48 тФВ rtk powershell -NoProfile -Command "`$target = Join-Path `$env:APPDATA 'kingsoft\wps\jsaddons'; Get-ChildItem -LiteralPath `$target -Directory | Select-Object Name,LastWriteTime"
 49 тФВ ```
 50 тФВ 
 51 тФВ For `$_` with outer double quotes, escape `$_` and quote regex alternation inside the script:
 52 тФВ 
 53 тФВ ```powershell
 54 тФВ rtk powershell -NoProfile -Command "Get-Process | Where-Object { `$_.ProcessName -match 'node|vite|wps|powershell' } | Select-Object Id,ProcessName,StartTime"
 55 тФВ ```
 56 тФВ 
 57 тФВ For nested quotes in WMI/CIM filters, assign the filter to a variable first:
 58 тФВ 
 59 тФВ ```powershell
 60 тФВ rtk powershell -NoProfile -Command "`$filter = 'Name = ''powershell.exe'''; Get-CimInstance Win32_Process -Filter `$filter | Select-Object ProcessId,CommandLine"
 61 тФВ ```
 62 тФВ 
 63 тФВ ## Paths And Files
 64 тФВ 
 65 тФВ - Use `-LiteralPath` for exact paths, especially paths with spaces, brackets, wildcards, or non-ASCII characters.
 66 тФВ - Quote all Windows paths.
 67 тФВ - Prefer `Join-Path` for paths built from `$env:APPDATA`, `$HOME`, or workspace variables.
 68 тФВ 
 69 тФВ Read a file:
 70 тФВ 
 71 тФВ ```powershell
 72 тФВ rtk powershell -NoProfile -Command 'Get-Content -LiteralPath "C:\path\file.txt"'
 73 тФВ ```
 74 тФВ 
 75 тФВ List selected files:
 76 тФВ 
 77 тФВ ```powershell
 78 тФВ rtk powershell -NoProfile -Command 'Get-ChildItem -LiteralPath "C:\path" -Force | Select-Object Name,LastWriteTime,Length'
 79 тФВ ```
 80 тФВ 
 81 тФВ Inspect generated or installed artifacts under `%APPDATA%`:
 82 тФВ 
 83 тФВ ```powershell
 84 тФВ rtk powershell -NoProfile -Command "`$asset = Join-Path `$env:APPDATA 'kingsoft\wps\jsaddons\wps-ai_1.0.4\assets'; Get-ChildItem -LiteralPath `$asset -Filter 'taskpane-*.js' | Select-Object Name,LastWriteTime,Length"
 85 тФВ ```
 86 тФВ 
 87 тФВ For timestamp checks where the target directory is already specific, list and sort the target directly instead of embedding complex regex filters:
 88 тФВ 
 89 тФВ ```powershell
 90 тФВ rtk powershell -NoProfile -Command '$target = Join-Path $env:APPDATA "kings
... (truncated)
```

# RTK - Rust Token Killer (Codex CLI)

**Usage**: Token-optimized CLI proxy for shell commands.

## Rule

Always prefix shell commands with `rtk`.

Examples:

```bash
rtk git status
rtk cargo test
rtk npm run build
rtk pytest -q
```

## Meta Commands

```bash
rtk gain            # Token savings analytics
rtk gain --history  # Recent command savings history
rtk proxy <cmd>     # Run raw command without filtering
```

## Verification

```bash
rtk --version
rtk gain
which rtk
```

---SKILL:wps-ai-testing---
---
name: wps-ai-testing
description: Use when running or troubleshooting WPS-AI-CHAT desktop UI tests in WPS Writer, especially tests that must send a prompt through the WPS AI taskpane and verify WPS document rendering for headings, body text, tables, and code highlighting.
---

# WPS AI Testing

Use the project test runner instead of ad hoc click commands.

## Entry Point

From `d:\cursor_dev\wps_ai`:

```powershell
rtk powershell -NoProfile -ExecutionPolicy Bypass -File ".\scripts\run-wps-ai-ui-test.ps1"
```

Options:

```powershell
rtk powershell -NoProfile -ExecutionPolicy Bypass -File ".\scripts\run-wps-ai-ui-test.ps1" -WaitSeconds 240
rtk powershell -NoProfile -ExecutionPolicy Bypass -File ".\scripts\run-wps-ai-ui-test.ps1" -NoPopup
rtk powershell -NoProfile -ExecutionPolicy Bypass -File ".\scripts\run-wps-ai-ui-test.ps1" -KeepExistingWps
```

## Rules

1. Do not start with loose `click-screen` commands. Run the runner first so every step is logged.
2. The runner shows a colored topmost status popup and writes logs under `test-artifacts\wps-ai-ui\<timestamp>\`.
3. By default the runner closes existing WPS Writer (`wps.exe`) windows before opening a fresh document; it must not close WPS Spreadsheets (`et.exe`).
4. The runner writes periodic screenshots under `captures\` while waiting for the taskpane and document output.
5. Save each test document as a unique ASCII `.docx` in the run folder before sending the prompt; this avoids stale `щПВхЫзчУзщПВхЫйюЯИ1` conversation history.
6. The fixed prompt is `scripts\wps-ai-render-test-prompt.txt`; read prompt files with .NET UTF-8 APIs, not plain `Get-Content`, when clipboard text may contain Chinese.
7. Use recursive UI Automation traversal to find `CefBrowserWindow` or `KxJSCTPWidget`; single-call `FindFirst` can miss nested WPS/CEF panes.
8. Submit prompts by focusing the textarea, pressing `Ctrl+A`, pasting UTF-8 clipboard text, and pressing `Enter`; use button clicks only as a fallback.
9. If output does not appear before timeout, stop and inspect `run.log`, `summary.json`, `captures\`, and `stuck.png`; do not repeat the same send command.
10. Leave the generated WPS document open for manual inspection.

## Validation Targets

The test must check:

- AI taskpane prompt submission.
- WPS body text output length.
- At least one real WPS table with expected dimensions.
- Code font and colored code characters.
- Heading-like paragraphs, bold, and italic formatting.
- Screenshots before send and after output.

---SKILL:windows-powershell---
---
name: windows-powershell
description: Use when composing or running Windows PowerShell commands from Codex, especially through `rtk powershell`, `powershell -Command`, `powershell -File`, commands with `$_`, pipes, quotes, paths with spaces, environment variables, or scripts. Prevents repeated quoting, escaping, and script invocation mistakes on Windows.
---

# Windows PowerShell

Use this skill before running non-trivial PowerShell commands on Windows. Keep updates concise: add only correct reusable command forms and decision rules, not failing commands or incident notes.

## Command Review

Before executing, check:

- Entry mode: `rtk powershell -Command` for inline script, `rtk powershell -File` only for `.ps1`.
- Write scope: target files, install directories, generated artifacts, processes.
- Fragile tokens: `$_`, `$env:...`, `|`, `>`, `<`, `&`, `;`, quotes, paths with spaces.
- Whether a small read-only probe should run before a write/copy/remove/build/install command.

## Entry Rules

- Run PowerShell scripts as:

```powershell
rtk powershell -NoProfile -ExecutionPolicy Bypass -File ".\scripts\task.ps1"
```

- Run `.py`, `.js`, `.mjs`, `.bat`, or `.cmd` with their own executable, not `powershell -File`.
- Use `rtk <program> ...` directly when PowerShell is unnecessary, for example `rtk git status`, `rtk npm test`, `rtk node script.mjs`.
- For `.ps1` scripts that may run under Windows PowerShell 5, keep parameter defaults and control strings ASCII, or save explicitly as UTF-8 with BOM; otherwise non-ASCII defaults can be decoded incorrectly.
- For UTF-8 prompt or fixture files that may contain Chinese, read with `.NET` encoding APIs: `[System.IO.File]::ReadAllText($path, [System.Text.UTF8Encoding]::new($false, $true))`.
- In PowerShell functions that return objects, do not use `Write-Output` for logging; it pollutes the success pipeli
... (truncated)
```
</details></tool-use>

<tool-use data-tool-type="shell" data-tool-name="shell_command"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **shell_command** `rtk read -n --max-lines 220 scripts/README-wps-ai-ui-test.md`</summary>
```
Exit code: 0
Wall time: 0.6 seconds
Output:
 1 тФВ # WPS AI UI Test
 2 тФВ 
 3 тФВ Use one entry point for WPS desktop testing:
 4 тФВ 
 5 тФВ ```powershell
 6 тФВ rtk powershell -NoProfile -ExecutionPolicy Bypass -File ".\scripts\run-wps-ai-ui-test.ps1"
 7 тФВ ```
 8 тФВ 
 9 тФВ The runner creates a timestamped folder under `test-artifacts/wps-ai-ui/` with:
10 тФВ 
11 тФВ - `run.log`: full step log with timestamps.
12 тФВ - `status.json`: current status consumed by the popup.
13 тФВ - `summary.json`: table, code highlighting, heading, and body checks.
14 тФВ - `before-send.png`, `after-output.png`, and `stuck.png` when applicable.
15 тФВ - `captures/`: periodic screenshots while waiting for the taskpane and generated document output.
16 тФВ 
17 тФВ Rules for this test flow:
18 тФВ 
19 тФВ - Do not send ad hoc click commands first. Run the test runner so progress and failures are logged.
20 тФВ - The runner closes existing WPS Writer (`wps.exe`) windows before each run by default; it does not close WPS Spreadsheets (`et.exe`).
21 тФВ - The runner saves each test document as a unique ASCII `.docx` under the run folder to avoid reusing stale `цЦЗхнЧцЦЗчи┐1` sessions.
22 тФВ - Keep `.ps1` defaults ASCII. Read prompt files with .NET UTF-8 APIs.
23 тФВ - Use recursive UI Automation traversal to find `CefBrowserWindow` or `KxJSCTPWidget`; single-call `FindFirst` can miss nested WPS/CEF panes.
24 тФВ - Submit prompts by focusing the textarea, pressing `Ctrl+A`, pasting UTF-8 clipboard text, and pressing `Enter`; use button clicks only as a fallback.
25 тФВ - If output does not appear before timeout, stop and inspect the log/screenshot instead of repeating the same send command.
26 тФВ - Leave the WPS document open after the run for manual inspection.
```
</details></tool-use>

   1: param(
   2:   [string]$TitleLike = "WPS",

   6:   [int]$InputY = 0,
   7:   [int]$SendX = 0,

   9:   [ValidateSet("Enter", "Click")]
  10:   [string]$SubmitMode = "Enter"
